black_asp.name: Black Asp
description: ## Black Asp
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/67›

The black asps are a sinister order of monks who train as assassins and infiltrators with no need for weapons to achieve their goals. Through meditation and study, they learn how to use poison, hide any magics they choose to employ, and even empty their minds of thought to protect themselves from divinations.

::ab[Black Asp's Path (Ex)]{icon=def-power ability="A black asp is trained in the use of poison and cannot accidentally poison himself when applying poison to a weapon or when rolling a natural 1 on an attack roll with a poisoned weapon." imp3="The black asp's ability to use poison improves. He gains ‹feat/Adder Strike› as a bonus feat even if he does not meet the prerequisites." replace="Stunning fist~still mind"}

::ab[Forbidden Powers (Su)]{head icon=power ability="A black asp can select a *forbidden ki power* in place of any of the following monk class abilities: *slow fall* (4th), *high jump* (5th), *wholeness of body* (7th), *diamond body* (11th), *abundant step* (12th), *diamond soul* (13th), *quivering palm* (15th), *tongue of the sun and moon* (17th), and *empty body* (19th). If he chooses to do so, he replaces the monk class ability the black asp gives up for the chosen *forbidden ki power.* The black asp can select any of the following *forbidden ki powers.*"}

::ab[Black Asp Venom (Su)]{next prereq="12th-level monk" icon=power swift="A black asp with this ki power can poison his unarmed strike or a weapon in his hand. The poison lasts for 1 minute or until expended. A creature damaged by the poisoned weapon is affected as per the ‹spell/poison› spell. The save DC is equal to 10 + 1/2 the black asp's monk level + his Wisdom modifier. Each use of this ability costs 2 ki points."}

::ab[Hidden Magic (Sp)]{next icon=def immediate="The black asp can spend 1 ki point to hide the auras of all magic items he is carrying (as if they were all under ‹spell/magic aura›) for 1 hour per monk level."}

::ab[Greater Hidden Magic (Sp)]{next prereq="8th-level monk, *hidden magic* ki power" order=pi icon=def passive="As long as he has at least 1 point in his *ki pool,* all of the black asp's items are constantly under the effects of *magic aura.*" immediate="He can spend 2 ki points to cause himself and any magic items he is carrying to appear to be nonmagical (as if he had cast ‹spell/greater magic aura›) for 1 hour per monk level."}

::ab[Empty Mind (Sp)]{next icon=def ability="A black asp with this ki power can choose to lower the number of ki points in his *ki pool* by 3 when he recovers his ki points for the day. If he does so, he gains the benefits of ‹spell/mind blank› for 24 hours." prereq="16th-level monk, *greater hidden magic* ki power"}
::ab[Shifting Form (Su)]{next icon=def immediate="A black asp can spend 1 ki point to make his outline shift and waver for 1 round. This ability otherwise functions as per the spell ‹spell/blur›."}

black_asp_uc.name: Black Asp (Unchained)
regex: in place of any of the.+the chosen \*forbidden

brazen_disciple.name: Brazen Disciple
description: ## Brazen Disciple
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/104›

Of the three monasteries, the Monastery of Unblinking Flame has been the most successful in developing a specific and unique fighting style that sets its disciples apart from other monks. In order to be admitted into the Monastery of Unblinking Flame and become one of these disciples, a supplicant must prove his worth by outwitting an ‹monster/efreeti›. Those who succeed often become monks known as the brazen disciples.

Brazen disciples believe that the best way to prepare for this test is to emulate the efreeti's own duplicitous ways. By studying genie contracts, brazen disciples learn how to take advantage of loopholes, and by pondering riddles, they learn how to obfuscate truth. They use deception in their fighting style, and many go a step further, incorporating fire and smoke both mundane and magical. Though most brazen disciples focus on joining the ‹rule/Houses of Perfection›, some become enamored with the efreet and instead seek out genie masters.
::cskill{gain=bluff~dis~kp lose=kr~ride~swim}

::ab[Feinting Flurry (Ex)]{icon=melee-lower ability="When using *flurry of blows* to make melee attacks, a brazen disciple can forgo his first attack to attempt a Bluff check to feint." imp6="When a brazen disciple successfully feints, his opponent is denied its Dexterity bonus to AC until the end of the monk's turn." replace="Bonus feats gained at 1st level and at 6th level"}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power l2="At 2nd, 10th, 14th, and 18th levels, a brazen disciple can select a bonus feat. These feats must be chosen from the following list: ‹feat/Catch Off-Guard›, ‹feat/Combat Reflexes›, ‹feat/Deceitful›, ‹feat/Dodge›, ‹feat/Elemental Fist›, and ‹feat/Improved Initiative›." l6="The brazen disciple can also choose from the following feats: ‹feat/Efreeti Style›, ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick›, ‹feat/Improved Disarm›, ‹feat/Improved Steal›, ‹feat/Improved Trip›, and ‹feat/Mobility›." l10="The brazen disciple can also choose from the following feats: ‹feat/Efreeti Stance›, ‹feat/Efreeti Touch›, ‹feat/Improved Critical›, and ‹feat/Spring Attack›." special="He need not meet the prerequisites normally required for these feats to select them." alter="Bonus feats"}
::ab[Efreeti's Guile (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="A brazen disciple can add his Wisdom modifier on Bluff and Disguise checks instead of his Charisma modifier." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Confounding Koan (Sp)]{l=12 icon=magic standard="A brazen disciple can spend 2 ki points to confuse a target within 30 feet with a riddle or paradox. This ability functions as per ‹spell/confusion›, except that it is language-dependent. The DC is equal to 10 + half the monk's class level + his Wisdom modifier, and the caster level is equal to his class level." replace="Abundant step"}

::ab[Genie Apotheosis]{l=20 icon=magic-power passive="A brazen disciple is treated as a native outsider with the fire subtype rather than as a humanoid (or whatever the monk's creature type was) for the purpose of spells and magical effects. He has immunity to fire and vulnerability to cold." order=pa ability="Once per day, the brazen disciple can grant a ‹spell/limited wish› to a non-outsider as a spell-like ability (CL 20th)." replace="Perfect self"}

disciple_of_wholeness.name: Disciple of Wholeness
description: ## Disciple of Wholeness
‹SOURCE Healer's Handbook/4›
Disciples of wholeness train to bring themselves and others closer to physical and spiritual perfection.

::ab[Healing Ki (Su)]{l=4 icon=aid swift="A disciple of wholeness can touch a creature and spend 1 point from her *ki pool* to restore a number of hit points equal to 1d4 + 1/2 her monk level to that creature. If she has the *wholeness of body* class feature, she can spend 2 points to restore a number of hit points equal to 1d8 + her monk level to a touched creature." alter="Ki pool" replace="The ability to spend ki to gain an extra attack during a *flurry of blows*"}

::ab[Hone Body (Su)]{l=5 icon=def order=pw passive="A disciple of wholeness has immunity to supernatural and magical diseases as long as she is undamaged (she loses this immunity when she has hit point or ability score damage)." swift="She can touch a creature and spend 1 point from her *ki pool* to render it immune to all diseases for 1 day. She cannot grant this immunity if the target is already afflicted with a disease." replace="Purity of body"}

::ab[Greater Hone Body (Su)]{l=11 icon=def ability="A disciple of wholeness is immune to poison while undamaged and can grant immunity to poison (lasting for 1 day). This ability otherwise functions as per *hone body.*" replace="Diamond body"}

::ab[Hone Soul (Su)]{l=13 icon=magic standard="A disciple of wholeness can dispel a spell affecting a living creature by touching it and spending 1 point from her *ki pool.* This power functions like the targeted dispel option of ‹spell/greater dispel magic›, with the caster level equal to the disciple of wholeness's monk level." replace="Diamond soul"}

disciple_of_wholeness_uc.name: Disciple of Wholeness (Unchained)
regex: The ability to spend ki to gain an extra attack during a \*flurry of blows\*
12th-level ki power~ability to select the ‹kipower/diamond body› ki power
14th-level ki power~ability to select the ‹kipower/diamond soul› ki power

drunken_master.name: Drunken Master
description: ## Drunken Master
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/110›

Most monks lead lives of moderation and quiet contemplation. But the drunken master finds perfection through excess. Powered by strong wine, he uses his intoxication to reach a state where his ki is more potent, if somewhat fleeting. A drunken master has the following class features.

::ab[Drunken Ki (Su)]{l=3 icon=power provokes=No standard="A drunken master can drink a tankard of ale or strong alcohol and gain one temporary ki point. This does not provoke attacks of opportunity. The monk can have a maximum of 1 *drunken ki point.*" special="The monk can gain this temporary ki even before he gains a *ki pool* at 4th level. These *drunken ki points* last for 1 hour or until spent, whichever is shorter." order=sw swift="As long as he has at least 1 *drunken ki point,* the monk can spend 1 ki point to move 5 feet without provoking attacks of opportunity." replace="Still mind" incrementPlain="The monk's maximum increases~3~2" incrementDesc=to incrementEnd=" *drunken ki points.*"}

::ab[Drunken Strength (Su)]{l=5 icon=melee-boost swift="A drunken master can spend 1 point of ki to inflict 1d6 extra points of damage on a single successful melee attack. The monk can choose to apply the damage after the attack roll is made." incrementMulti="The monk may spend ~ *drunken ki points* to increase the extra damage to ~d6.~5/5;2/1" special="The monk must have at least 1 *drunken ki point* to use this ability." replace="Purity of body"}
::ab[Drunken Courage (Su)]{l=11 icon=def passive="A drunken master is immune to fear as long as he has at least 1 point of *drunken ki.*" replace="Diamond body"}

::ab[Drunken Resilience (Ex)]{l=13 icon=def passive="A drunken master gains ‹rule/DR› 1/- as long as he has at least 1 point of *drunken ki.*" imp16="The DR increases to 2/-." imp19="It increases to 3/-." replace="Diamond soul"}

::ab[Firewater Breath (Su)]{l=19 icon=cone standard="A drunken master can take a drink and expel a gout of alcohol-fueled fire in a 30-foot cone. Creatures within the cone take 20d6 points of fire damage. A successful Reflex saving throw (DC 10 + 1/2 the monk's level + the monk's Wis modifier) halves the damage. Using this ability consumes 4 ki points from the monk's *ki pool.*" special="The monk must have at least 1 *drunken ki point* to use this ability." replace="Empty body"}

elemental_monk.name: Elemental Monk
description: ## Elemental Monk
‹SOURCE Disciple's Doctrine/4›
Elemental monks draw inspiration from genies to create their fighting style. Their adaptability and versatility make them capable of serving as emissaries of elemental balance.
::ab[Alignment]{icon=info note="An elemental monk must be true neutral." replace="Alignment requirement"}

::ab[Elemental Strike (Ex)]{icon=power ability="An elemental monk gains ‹feat/Elemental Fist› as a bonus feat, even if she doesn't meet the prerequisites. She does not have to select an element; instead, each time she hits with an Elemental Fist attack, she can choose acid, cold, electricity, or fire for the type of the additional damage granted by that feat." replace="All bonus feats~stunning fist"}

::ab[Genie Style (Ex, Sp)]{icon=power replace="Evasion~purity of body~diamond body" l2="As a @HLswift_action an elemental monk can gain the benefits of one of the following style feats, based on one of the five types of genies. When she does so, she automatically enters that feat's stance: ‹feat/Djinni Style›, ‹feat/Efreeti Style›, ‹feat/Janni Style›, ‹feat/Marid Style›, or ‹feat/Shaitan Style›." l6="While the elemental monk is using a genie style feat, she also gains the benefits of a feat from the following list, as appropriate for that style's feat path: ‹feat/Djinni Spirit›, ‹feat/Efreeti Stance›, ‹feat/Janni Tempest›, ‹feat/Marid Spirit›, and ‹feat/Shaitan Skin›." l10="While the elemental monk is using a genie style feat, she also gains the benefits of a feat from the following list, as appropriate for that style's feat path: ‹feat/Djinni Spin›, ‹feat/Efreeti Touch›, ‹feat/Janni Rush›, ‹feat/Marid Coldsnap›, and ‹feat/Shaitan Earthblast›." l18="When the elemental monk gains the benefits of Djinni Spirit, Efreeti Stance, Marid Spirit, or Shaitan Skin, her elemental resistance from that feat instead becomes immunity to that element, as long as she is not denied her Dexterity bonus to AC. While she is using Janni Style, she gains the effects of ‹spell/enlarge person› or ‹spell/reduce person› (her choice), even if she is not humanoid. This is a spell-like ability with a caster level equal to the elemental monk's level."}

::ab[Elemental Precision (Ex)]{l=10 icon=down-lower ability="Instead of treating her unarmed strikes as lawful for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction, an elemental monk overcomes all damage reduction of creatures with the air, earth, fire, or water subtypes except DR/- and DR/epic." alter="Ki pool"}

::ab[Planar Guide (Sp)]{l=14 icon=magic standard="An elemental monk can spend 1 ki point to transport herself and up to seven additional willing creatures, as per the ‹spell/plane shift› spell, to one of the four Elemental Planes. Each additional creature increases the cost by 1 ki point."}

far_strike_monk.name: Far Strike Monk
description: ## Far Strike Monk
‹SOURCE Ranged Tactics Toolbox/9›

Far strike monks are masters of thrown weapons, from ‹eq-weapon/shuriken› to ‹eq-weapon/throwing axe«s› to ‹eq-weapon/spear«s›. The far strike school views thrown attacks as an extension of unarmed strikes, and masters of this school can infuse thrown weapons with their ki.
::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power note="In addition to the standard monk weapon proficiencies, a far strike monk is proficient with all thrown weapons."}

::ab[Flurry of Blows (Ex)]{icon=ranged-down ability="A far strike monk can make a *flurry of blows* as a full-attack action as long as he's attacking with thrown weapons. He can't make a *flurry of blows* with his unarmed attacks or any other weapons. A far strike monk's *flurry of blows* otherwise functions as the standard monk class feature." special="A far strike monk can't use ‹feat/Rapid Shot› when making a *flurry of blows* with a thrown weapon." alter="Flurry of blows"}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power l1="A far strike monk adds the following feats to his list of bonus feats: ‹feat/Far Shot›, ‹feat/Point-Blank Shot›, ‹feat/Precise Shot›, and any ‹main/targeting »feat<s_optional_rules›." l6="He adds the following feats: ‹feat/Improved Precise Shot›, ‹feat/Parting Shot›, and ‹feat/Trick Shooter›." l10="He adds the following feats: ‹feat/Improvised Weapon Mastery› and ‹feat/Pinpoint Targeting›."}

::ab[Fast Thrower]{icon=power-ranged l1="A far strike monk gains ‹feat/Quick Draw› as a bonus feat, even if he doesn't meet the prerequisites." l4="He gains ‹feat/Shot on the Run› as a bonus feat, even if he doesn't meet the prerequisites." l8="When using the Shot on the Run feat, he can make a second ranged attack with a -5 penalty at any point during his movement." l15="He can make a third ranged attack with a -10 penalty at any point during his movement." replace="Stunning Fist"}

::ab[Invisible Blade (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="When a far strike monk uses the Stealth skill to maintain his obscured location after sniping, he takes a -10 penalty on his Stealth check instead of -20." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Ki Pool (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="In addition to the normal abilities of his *ki pool,* a far strike monk can spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to increase the range increment of a thrown weapon by 20 feet for 1 round."}

::ab[Ki Missile (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost swift="A far strike monk can spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to change the base damage dice of thrown weapons to that of his unarmed strikes. These weapons are also treated as ‹magic-enh/ki focus› weapons, allowing the monk to apply his special ki attacks to his thrown weapons as if they were unarmed strikes. This effect lasts until the beginning of his next turn." replace="Purity of body"}

::ab[Trick Throw (Su)]{l=11 icon=lower flavor="A far strike monk can infuse his thrown weapons with ki to hit targets that he might otherwise miss." swift="By spending 1 point from his *ki pool,* the far strike monk can ignore ‹rule/concealment›. By spending 2 points, he can ignore total concealment or ‹rule/cover›. By spending 3 points, he can ignore total cover, even throwing weapons around corners. These effects last for 1 round." special="The weapon must still be able to reach the target; for instance, a target inside a closed building with no open doors or windows cannot be attacked using Trick Throw." replace="Diamond body"}

flowing_monk.name: Flowing Monk
description: ## Flowing Monk
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/58›

The flowing monk is the wind and the river. He knows how the world flows, and forces his enemies to flow with it. Even the most powerful stone breaks under the graceful and persistent pressure of wind and water.

::ab[Bonus Feat]{icon=power l1="A flowing monk replaces the normal monk bonus feats with the following: ‹feat/Agile Maneuvers›, ‹feat/Combat Reflexes›, ‹feat/Deflect Arrows›, ‹feat/Dodge›, ‹feat/Improved Reposition›, ‹feat/Improved Trip›, ‹feat/Nimble Moves›, and ‹feat/Weapon Finesse›." l6="The following feats are also available: ‹feat/Acrobatic Steps›, ‹feat/Bodyguard›, ‹feat/Improved Disarm›, ‹feat/Improved Feint›, ‹feat/Ki Throw›, ‹feat/Mobility›, ‹feat/Second Chance›, and ‹feat/Sidestep›." l10="The following feats are added: ‹feat/In Harm's Way›, ‹feat/Repositioning Strike›, ‹feat/Snatch Arrows›, ‹feat/Spring Attack›, and ‹feat/Tripping Strike›."}

::ab[Redirection (Ex)]{l=1 icon=melee immediate="A flowing monk can attempt a ‹rule/reposition› or ‹rule/trip› combat maneuver against a creature that the flowing monk threatens and that attacks him. If the combat maneuver is successful, the attacker is ‹misc/sickened› for a number of rounds equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fourth› of her monk level (Reflex DC = 10 + 1/2 the monk's level + monk's Wisdom modifier to halve the duration). The monk gains a +2 bonus on the reposition or trip combat maneuver check and the save DC for redirection increases by 2 if the attacker is using ‹feat/Power Attack› or is ‹rule/charging› when attacking him. The benefit increases to a +4 bonus and an increase of the saving throw by 4 if both apply." imp4="A flowing monk can use redirection against an opponent that the flowing monk threatens and that attacks an ally with a melee attack." imp8="A flowing monk can make both a reposition and a trip maneuver as part of a single immediate action with this ability." imp12="A flowing monk can use redirection against any opponent that attacks him in melee, even if the flowing monk is not threatening the opponent who attacks him." special="A flowing monk can use this ability once per day per monk level, but no more than once per round." replace="Stunning fist"}

::ab[Unbalancing Counter (Ex)]{l=2 icon=lower ability="A flowing monk's attacks of opportunity render a struck creature ‹rule/flat-footed› until the end of the flowing monk's next turn (Reflex DC 10 + 1/2 the monk's level + Wisdom modifier negates)." replace="The bonus feat gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Flowing Dodge (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A flowing monk gains a +1 dodge bonus to AC for each enemy adjacent to him, up to a maximum bonus equal to his Wisdom modifier (minimum 1)." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Elusive Target (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def immediate="A flowing monk may spend 2 points from his *ki pool* to attempt a Reflex save opposed by an attacker's attack roll to halve damage from that attack." imp11="The flowing monk suffers no damage on a successful save, or half damage on a failed save. If the attacker is ‹rule/flanking› the monk, the flanking opponent who is not attacking becomes the target of the attack. Use the same attack roll, and if the attack hits the new target, that creatures takes half damage (or full damage if the attack is completely avoided). Any associated effects from the attack (such as ‹umr/bleed›, poison, or spell effects) apply fully even if the attack deals only half damage." replace="Purity of body~diamond body"}

::ab[Volley Spell (Su)]{l=15 icon=def ability="When a targeted spell or spell-like ability fails to overcome a flowing monk's spell resistance, he may reflect the effect onto its caster as ‹spell/spell turning› by spending a number of points from his *ki pool* equal to 1/2 the spell's level (minimum 1)." replace="Quivering palm"}

gray_disciple.name: Gray Disciple
description: ## Gray Disciple
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/187›
The gray disciple contemplates the inner voice of duergar magic and the silent eternity of stone, mastering these dual mysteries and combining them to deadly effect.
::prereq{r=Duergar}

::ab[Fade from Sight (Sp)]{l=4 icon=magic swift="The gray disciple can become invisible (as the ‹spell/invisibility› spell) for 1 round by spending 1 ki point." special="To use this ability, the gray disciple must already have *invisibility* as a spell-like ability." replace="Slow fall~still mind"}

::ab[Gray Heart (Sp)]{l=6 icon=magic swift="The gray disciple can enlarge himself (as the ‹spell/enlarge person› spell) for 1 minute by spending 1 ki point. To use this ability, the gray disciple must already have *enlarge person* available as a spell-like ability." replace="High jump~bonus feat gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Born in Darkness (Sp)]{l=7 icon=magic-aura standard="The gray disciple can radiate ‹spell/darkness› (as the spell, except originating from the disciple's person) for 1 round per level by spending 1 ki point." replace="Wholeness of body"}

::ab[Earth Glide (Su)]{l=12 icon=magic swift="The gray disciple can spend a ki point to walk through solid stone for 1 round. This functions as the ‹umr/earth glide› universal monster ability. The gray disciple may continue *earth gliding* as long as he spends 1 ki point every round as a @HLswift_action. If he ceases *earth gliding* within a solid object, he is violently ejected and takes 5d6 points of damage. The gray disciple is not harmed by damage caused to material he is *earth gliding* through, but a ‹spell/stone to flesh› spell cast upon it causes violent ejection as described above." replace="Abundant step"}

::ab[Entomb (Su)]{l=15 icon=magic-melee ability="A gray disciple can phase a foe into solid rock, killing it instantly. To use this ability, he expends 1 ki point as part of a ‹rule/bull rush› or ‹rule/reposition› combat maneuver against a creature adjacent to unworked earth or stone. If the attempt succeeds, the gray disciple pushes his foe inside the rock using his *earth glide* ability. If the creature succeeds at a Reflex save (DC 10 + 1/2 the gray disciple's monk level + the gray disciple's Wisdom modifier), it is ejected in the nearest open space and takes 5d6 points of damage. If it fails, it dies instantly as its body merges with the surrounding stone." special="*Entomb* is usable once per day, but a failed bull rush or reposition attempt does not count as a use of the ability. *Entomb* has no effect on creatures that can *earth glide,* are ‹umr/incorporeal›, or can otherwise can survive merging with a solid object." replace="Quivering palm"}

::ab[Earthen Thrall (Sp)]{l=17 icon=magic ability="A gray disciple can attempt to control a creature with the earth subtype once per day. This ability is treated as ‹spell/dominate monster› (DC 10 + 1/2 the gray disciple's monk level + the gray disciple's Wisdom modifier), but is only effective against creatures with the earth subtype, and the gray disciple can keep only a single creature enthralled." special="If he attempts to control a second creature with this ability, the first creature is automatically released from domination whether or not the second attempt succeeds." replace="Tongue of the sun and moon~empty body"}

hamatulatsu_master.name: Hamatulatsu Master
description: ## Hamatulatsu Master
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/38›

Once part of the Sisterhood of Eiseth, the Sisterhood of the Golden Erinyes is an order of female monks dedicated to emulating the painful strikes of the ‹monster/barbed devil›, leaving targets alive but permanently scarred and broken by pain - a method called hamatulatsu. The sisters train equally hard to resist their own techniques, and nearly all revere ‹faith/Asmodeus›.
::ab[Alignment]{icon=info note="A hamatulatsu master must be lawful neutral or lawful evil."}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power l1="A hamatulatsu master replaces the normal monk bonus feats with the following: ‹feat/Deflect Arrows›, ‹feat/Dodge›, ‹feat/Exotic Weapon Proficiency›, ‹feat/Improved Grapple›, ‹feat/Intimidating Prowess›, ‹feat/Scorpion Style›, or ‹feat/Weapon Focus›." l6="The following feats are added to the list: ‹feat/Gorgon's Fist›, ‹feat/Hamatulatsu›, ‹feat/Improved Sunder›, ‹feat/Mobility›, ‹feat/Power Attack›." l10="The following feats are added to the list: ‹feat/Critical Focus›, ‹feat/Impaling Critical› (unarmed strike), ‹feat/Improved Critical›, ‹feat/Medusa's Wrath›, ‹feat/Snatch Arrows›." l14="The following feat is added to the list: ‹feat/Improved Impaling Critical›." special="Any time a hamatulatsu master would gain a bonus feat, she can instead choose to gain two additional uses of *stunning fist* per day."}

::ab[Stunning Fist (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A hamatulatsu master expands the conditions that she can apply to the target of her *stunning fist* beyond merely stunning her target." imp4="She can choose to make the target ‹misc/shaken› for 1 minute." imp8="She can choose to deal 1d10 points of nonlethal ‹umr/bleed› damage." imp12="She can choose to make the target ‹misc/frightened› for 1d3 rounds." imp16="She can deal 2d12 points of nonlethal bleed damage." special="The shaken and frightened conditions allow the target to attempt a Will save to negate the effect instead of a Fortitude save. This ability otherwise functions as normal for a monk of her level."}

::ab[Ki Pool (Su)]{icon=melee swift="By spending 1 point from her *ki pool,* a hamatulatsu master can make one additional attack at her highest attack bonus when making a *flurry of blows* attack. Alternatively, she can spend 1 ki point to gain one additional use of her *stunning fist* ability that round, though this does not grant her an additional attack." immediate="A hamatulatsu master can spend 1 ki point to make a single melee attack with a +2 bonus against a creature that has damaged her with a natural melee attack, unarmed strike, or weapon without the reach property." special="This ability otherwise functions as normal for a monk of her level." order=wi}

::ab[Infernal Resilience (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def passive="A hamatulatsu master gains immunity to all spells, spell-like abilities, and effects with the pain descriptor, as well as a +2 bonus on saving throws against effects that would sicken, nauseate, stagger, or stun her." replace="Purity of body"}

harrow_warden.name: Harrow Warden
description: ## Harrow Warden
‹SOURCE The Harrow Handbook/7›

Some who study the mysteries of the harrow do so to restore lost knowledge and pride to the Varisian people. The harrow warden fulfills this role as she seeks to bring her mind and body into alignment, and she protects her people by invoking the folklore that has guided them for so long. In mastering her own place in fate, a harrow warden learns how to misalign the destinies of others.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=info note="The harrow warden is proficient with all simple weapons, as well as with the ‹eq-armor/klar›, ‹eq-weapon/rapier›, ‹eq-weapon/short sword›, and ‹eq-weapon/starknife›. Additionally, harrow wardens treat starknives as monk weapons."}

::ab[Idiot Strike (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee useL=monk useUnit=time ability="The harrow warden can make an unarmed strike against a target and curse the creature with bad luck. Foes damaged by an *idiot strike* must succeed at a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 the monk's level + the monk's Wisdom modifier) or become jinxed. A jinxed creature must roll twice whenever it attempts an attack roll, a saving throw, a skill check, or a caster level check for 1 round, taking the lower result in each instance. She must declare that she is using an *idiot strike* before making the attack roll; thus, a failed roll wastes the attempt." imp4="The jinxed condition lasts for 2 rounds." imp12="The jinxed condition lasts for 3 rounds." imp16="A harrow warden can expend two uses of *idiot strike* to instead curse her target as the ‹spell/bestow curse› spell." replace="Stunning fist"}

::ab[Mute Hag Stance (Su)]{l=9 icon=lower flavor="The harrow warden can block opponents' metaphysical ties to one another." passive="Enemies threatened by the harrow warden do not gain the effects of any morale or insight bonuses on attack rolls or skill checks as long as they are within the harrow warden's threatened area." replace="Improved evasion"}

::ab[Big Sky Stance (Su)]{l=11 icon=def flavor="The harrow warden becomes secure in her role within the larger story." passive="She gains immunity to curses and polymorph effects." replace="Diamond body"}

::ab[Eclipse Strike (Su)]{l=15 icon=magic flavor="The harrow warden can strike a target with enough force to realign its place in the world." ability="Foes damaged by the harrow warden's *idiot strike* can attempt a Fortitude save (DC 10 + 1/2 the monk's level + the monk's Wisdom modifier). Those that fail are transmuted into a harmless form, as per the ‹spell/baleful polymorph› spell." replace="Quivering palm"}

hellcat.name: Hellcat
description: ## Hellcat
‹SOURCE Villain Codex/92›

Monks of the hellcat sect follow the teachings of Asmodeus or another infernal power. Their brutal training strips away their physical weaknesses, and their vicious fighting style is inspired by the predatory techniques of the hellcat.

::ab[Alignment]{icon=warning note="A hellcat must be lawful evil or lawful neutral. A hellcat who becomes lawful good or nonlawful cannot gain new levels as a monk but retains all monk abilities." alter="Alignment requirements"}
::cskill{gain=kp~sur lose=craft~kh~perf~prof}

::ab[Weapon Proficiency]{icon=info note="A hellcat is proficient with the ‹eq-weapon/club›, ‹eq-weapon/crossbow› (light or heavy), ‹eq-weapon/dagger›, ‹eq-weapon/kama›, ‹eq-weapon/light mace›, ‹eq-weapon/nunchaku›, ‹eq-weapon/quarterstaff›, ‹eq-weapon/repeating crossbow› (light or heavy), ‹eq-weapon/sai›, ‹eq-weapon/shuriken›, and ‹eq-weapon/siangham›. In addition to monk weapons and unarmed strikes, a hellcat can use a ‹eq-weapon/light mace› to perform a *flurry of blows*." replace="Weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{icon=power l1="A hellcat selects bonus feats from the following list: ‹feat/Aspect of the Beast›, ‹feat/Blind-Fight›, ‹feat/Combat Reflexes›, ‹feat/Dodge›, ‹feat/Diehard›, ‹feat/Improved Grapple›, and ‹feat/Power Attack›." l6="The following feats are added to the list: ‹feat/Critical Focus›, ‹feat/Greater Grapple›, ‹feat/Improved Blind-Fight›, ‹feat/Mobility›, and ‹feat/Stalwart›." l10="The following feats are added to the list: ‹feat/Bleeding Critical›, ‹feat/Greater Blind-Fight›, ‹feat/Improved Critical›, ‹feat/Improved Stalwart›, and ‹feat/Spring Attack›." alter="Bonus feat"}

::ab[Hellcat Fury (Ex)]{icon=melee l1="A hellcat can deal slashing damage with his unarmed strikes." l4="Whenever a hellcat confirms a critical hit with his slashing unarmed strike, the target of that attack also takes 1d4 points of ‹umr/bleed› damage." incrementMulti="The bleed damage increases to 1d~ points.~4/4;6/2" replace="Stunning fist~fast movement"}

::ab[Torture Training (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def ability="A hellcat who fails a save against an effect that causes him to become fatigued, sickened, or staggered can attempt a second save against the effect at the end of his next turn. Only one additional save is allowed." imp5="He can also attempt this second save against effects that would cause him to become frightened or shaken." imp9="He can also attempt this second save against effects that would cause him to become exhausted, nauseated, or dazed." replace="Evasion~purity of body~improved evasion"}

::ab[Crucible of Pain (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A hellcat gains an amount of ‹rule/DR›/- equal to half his monk level against nonlethal damage. In addition, a hellcat is inured to hot climate effects as if using ‹spell/endure elements›." replace="Slow fall~still mind"}

::ab[Hellcat Ki (Su)]{l=4 icon=power ability="A hellcat gains a pool of ki points that he can use as per the monk class feature but with the following changes. In addition to spending ki to gain an additional attack, increased speed, or improved AC, a hellcat can spend 1 ki point as a @HLswift_action to enhance his sight (gaining 60-foot darkvision) or his sense of smell (gaining the ‹umr/scent› ability) for 1 minute per level." imp7="A hellcat can spend 1 point of ki as a @HLswift_action to gain fire resistance 5 for 1 round per level." imp10="The fire resistance increases to 10." imp14="The fire resistance increases to 15." imp11="A hellcat can spend 1 point of ki as a @HLswift_action to become silhouetted with a ghostly flame, which makes him harder to see in light but easier to see in darkness for 1 round per level. He becomes invisible in bright light, gains ‹rule/concealment› (20% miss chance) in normal light, gains no benefit in dim light, and gives off light like a ‹eq-misc/candle› in darkness, allowing other creatures to see him even without darkvision. If he takes any action that would break ‹spell/invisibility› while in bright light, his invisibility is reduced to concealment until the end of his next turn." alter="Ki pool" replace="Wholeness of body~diamond body"}

hungry_ghost_monk.name: Hungry Ghost Monk
description: ## Hungry Ghost Monk
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/110›

The hungry ghost monk looks to spirits that prey upon the living as models of perfection. He sees the life energy of the universe as a resource to be manipulated, even stealing it from other creatures. It is through this constant influx of energy that the hungry ghost monk reaches his ultimate goal: power - personal, pure, and simple. A hungry ghost monk has the following class features.

::ab[Punishing Kick (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A hungry ghost monk gains ‹feat/Punishing Kick› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites." imp10="The monk can push the target of his Punishing Kick an additional 5 feet (to 10 feet)." imp15="He can push the target 15 feet away. Or, he can instead choose to push the target 5 feet and knock the target ‹rule/prone› with the same attack. The target still gets a saving throw to avoid being knocked prone." imp20="He can push the target 20 feet away." replace="Stunning Fist"}

::ab[Steal Ki (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power-def flavor="A hungry ghost monk can steal ki from other creatures, though this ability is controversial in some circles of monks, who see it as nothing less than a form of vampirism." ability="If the monk scores a confirmed critical hit against a living enemy or reduces a living enemy to 0 or fewer hit points, he can steal some of that creature's ki. This ability replenishes 1 spent ki point to the monk's *ki pool,* as long as the monk has at least 1 ki point in his pool. He cannot exceed his *ki pool's maximum." imp11="Each time the monk successfully steals ki, he can make an immediate saving throw against one disease he is suffering from. There is no penalty for failing this saving throw. The monk gains a bonus equal to his Wisdom modifier on the saving throw." replace="Purity of body"}

::ab[Life Funnel (Su)]{l=7 icon=aid flavor="A hungry ghost monk can steal a creature's life force to replenish his own." ability="If the monk has at least 1 ki point in his *ki pool* and scores a confirmed critical hit against a living enemy or reduces a living enemy to 0 or fewer hit points, he heals a number of hit points equal to his monk level. As with *steal ki,* some monks believe that *life funnel* is an unsavory act, no better than what the undead do to the living." special="A monk with this ability cannot steal both ki and hit points at the same time." replace="Wholeness of body"}

::ab[Life from a Stone (Su)]{l=11 icon=def-aid flavor="A hungry ghost monk can steal ki or life force from any creature, not just living creatures." ability="If the monk has at least 1 ki point in his pool, he gains the benefit of *life funnel* and *steal ki* when he confirms a critical hit against any creature or reduces any creature to 0 or fewer hit points." replace="Diamond body"}

::ab[Sipping Demon (Su)]{l=13 icon=aid ability="A hungry ghost monk gains 1 ‹rule/temporary hit point<s› each time he hits an enemy with a melee attack. The monk gains a number of temporary hit points equal to his Wisdom modifier when he scores a critical hit. The maximum number of temporary hit points the monk can have is equal to his monk level. The temporary hit points disappear 1 hour later." special="The monk can only use this ability when he has at least 1 ki point in his *ki pool.* This ability is a proscribed manipulation of ki considered by many good monks to be a corruption." replace="Diamond soul"}

invested_regent.name: Invested Regent
description: ## Invested Regent
‹SOURCE Heroes of the High Court/22›

The invested regent has been handpicked by inscrutable divine forces to command others - either at the present or at some future time. The invested regent can harness a divine spark to perform superhuman stunts, influence others, and escape injury. Invested regents come from all races and walks of life, but they are more likely to be nobleborn in their cultures. Although some invested regents are acutely aware of their powers' divine origins (such as powers gained from an investment ceremony to ‹faith/Asmodeus› in the nation of Cheliax), others do not know the source of their abilities. Invested regents have strong personalities and a destiny to rule.

::ab[Investiture (Su)]{icon=power-def clear order=aw ability="An invested regent gains a pool of *investiture points,* supernatural energy he can use to call upon amazing abilities and divine protection. The number of points in the invested regent's *investiture pool* is equal to 1/2 his monk level + his Charisma modifier." swift="As long as he has at least 1 point in his *investiture pool,* the invested regent can select one of his saving throws and gain a +1 sacred bonus on saving throws of that type for 1 round. If he spends 1 point from his *investiture pool* during this swift action, he instead gains a sacred bonus equal to his Charisma modifier on the saving throw selected." special="A character with this feature and the *ki pool* class feature tracks *investiture points* and ki points separately." replace="Bonus feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Vested Power (Ex or Sp)]{l=2 icon=power ability="An invested regent can select a vested power (see the Vested Powers section below) for which he qualifies in place of selecting a monk bonus feat. The invested regent need not do so and can instead take the bonus feat, but once the decision to take a bonus feat or a vested power is made, he can't change it." replace="Bonus feat the invested regent gives up for the vested power" incrementOrd="He can select~2~4" incrementDesc=a incrementEnd=" vested power."}
:::ab{title="Vested Powers" icon=info action=Info head}
Vested powers are abilities that draw on an invested regent's *investiture pool.* Vested powers are divided into two categories: feats and spells.
All vested powers have a minimum level requirement to select them. An invested regent who hasn't reached the required monk level cannot select that vested power.

Most vested powers require the invested regent to spend *investiture points;* the exact amount is listed after the vested power. Vested powers that cost 0 *investiture points* don't require the invested regent to have any *investiture points* in his *investiture pool* to use the ability. The saving throw against an invested regent's vested power, if any, is equal to 10 + 1/2 the invested regent's monk level + his Charisma bonus.

::ab[Feats]{next icon=info note="These vested powers duplicate the effects of specific feats. An invested regent doesn't need to qualify for a feat to select it as a vested power. For example, an invested regent can select Spring Attack as a vested power even if he doesn't meet the prerequisites for selecting Spring Attack as a feat. Activating one of these vested powers is a @HLfree_action on the invested regent's turn; until the start of his next turn, the invested regent is treated as if he had that feat. Some feats can also be activated as an @HLimmediate_action when it isn't the invested regent's turn."}

::ab[Spells]{next icon=info note="These vested powers duplicate the effects of a spell and are spell-like abilities. An invested regent's monk level is the caster level for these spell-like abilities, and he uses Charisma to determine his ‹rule/concentration› check bonus. Activating one of these vested powers is a @HLstandard_action"}
