ironskin_monk.name: Ironskin Monk
description: ## Ironskin Monk
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/122›
Through discipline and training, an ironskin monk hardens his body to withstand punishing blows. Though slow on his feet, his calloused hands and feet can shatter stone and stagger foes.
::prereq{r=Hobgoblin}

::ab[Iron Skin (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def passive="An ironskin monk gains a bonus to his natural armor equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fourth› of his monk level. This bonus stacks with any existing natural armor the ironskin monk already has." replace="AC bonus~the ability to add his Wisdom bonus to his AC"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{icon=power l1="An ironskin monk adds ‹feat/Power Attack› to his list of bonus feats." l6="He adds ‹feat/Improved Sunder› to the list." l10="He adds ‹feat/Greater Sunder› to the list." replace="‹feat/Dodge›, ‹feat/Mobility›, and ‹feat/Spring Attack› on the monk's bonus feat list"}

::ab[Resilience (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def flavor="An ironskin monk can shake off the physical effects of certain attacks." ability="If he makes a Fortitude saving throw against an attack that has a reduced effect on a successful save, he instead avoids the effect entirely." special="This ability can be used only if the monk is wearing light armor or no armor. A helpless monk does not gain the benefits of *resilience.*" replace=Evasion}

::ab[Ki Pool (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="An ironskin monk can spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to gain a damage bonus equal to 1/2 his level against objects and constructs for 1 round." replace="The ability to increase speed with ki"}

::ab[Staggering Blow (Ex)]{l=5 icon=lower free="An ironskin monk attacking with an unarmed strike can spend 1 point from his *ki pool* after a successful critical hit to ‹misc/stagger› the creature struck for 1 round (Fort DC 10 + 1/2 the ironskin monk's level + the ironskin monk's Wisdom modifier negates)." replace="High jump"}
::ab[Tough as Nails (Ex)]{l=6 icon=def passive="An ironskin monk gains ‹rule/DR› 1/-." incrementPlain="The DR~6~3" incrementEnd="/-." replace="Fast movement~slow fall"}
::ab[Evasion (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def ability="An ironskin monk gains ‹ability/evasion›." replace="Improved evasion"}
::ab[Surefooted (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power passive="An ironskin monk's speed is not reduced by ‹rule/difficult terrain›." replace="Tongue of the sun and moon"}

::ab[Unbreakable (Ex)]{l=20 icon=def passive="An ironskin monk sets aside many of the frailties of mortal flesh. He becomes immune to death effects and stunning. He is not subject to ‹rule/ability damage« or ability drain›, and has a 75% chance of ignoring the extra damage dealt by critical hits and sneak attacks." replace="Perfect self"}

karmic_monk.name: Karmic Monk
description: ## Karmic Monk
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/122›

A karmic monk contemplates the myriad harmonies that guide existence. He learns to use the disharmony in others against them, and to alter his own inner harmonies to exploit weaknesses in his opponents' defenses.
::ab[Alignment]{icon=info note="A karmic monk must be true neutral or of any lawful alignment." alter="Alignment requirement"}
::cskill{gain=diplo lose=intm}

::ab[Karmic Strike (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="A karmic monk gains bonuses against creatures that attack him first. If a creature the karmic monk has not attacked within the past 24 hours makes an attack roll against the karmic monk or casts an offensive spell that affects the karmic monk, the karmic monk receives a +2 bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls against that creature. If the karmic monk hits such a target, this bonus lasts until the end of the karmic monk's turn (or until the beginning of the karmic monk's next turn, if it isn't his turn). Otherwise, the bonus against a particular creature lasts for 1 minute after the creature's last attack against the karmic monk." replace="Stunning fist"}

::ab[Balanced Mind (Su)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A karmic monk receives a +2 bonus on saving throws against effects with the chaos, evil, good, or law subtype. He also receives this bonus against the abilities and effects of creatures of the listed subtypes." replace="Still mind"}
::ab[Ki Pool (Su)]{l=7 icon=boost passive="When a karmic monk makes unarmed strikes, they're treated as chaotic, evil, good, and lawful." replace="7th- and 10th-level *ki pool* abilities"}

::ab[Harmony (Su)]{l=9 icon=magic immediate="A karmic monk can shift his alignment by one step for the purpose of all effects dependent on alignment until the beginning of his next turn. This does not actually alter the karmic monk's alignment." replace="Improved evasion"}

::ab[Karmic Disruption (Su)]{l=16 icon=boost swift="A karmic monk can spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to add 1d6 points of damage to his unarmed strikes against creatures of either chaotic, evil, good, or lawful alignment for 1 round. This stacks with other abilities that deal damage based on alignment. He cannot alter his alignment choice until the next time he uses this ability. Creatures that are of the appropriate alignment and are struck by this attack must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the karmic monk's class level + the karmic monk's Wisdom modifier) or lose any damage reduction based on alignment for a number of rounds equal to the karmic monk's Wisdom modifier (minimum 1 round). A creature that succeeds at this save is immune to having its damage reduction disrupted by that karmic monk for 24 hours." special="This attack disrupts only alignment components of damage reduction, not any other components; for example, DR/cold iron and good becomes DR/cold iron." replace="The *slow fall* improvement; it remains at 70 feet"}
::ab[Slow Fall]{l=18 alter="Slow fall" ability="*Slow fall* now reduces your fall damage by 80 feet.@FN1" icon=boost}

::ab[Harmonic Self]{l=20 icon=down-def ability="A karmic monk is truly in tune with the harmonic balance of the universe. He gains the abilities of *perfect self,* except that instead of DR 10/chaotic, he gains DR 10 that is overcome only by attacks that do not count as chaotic, evil, good, or lawful." alter="Perfect self"}
:::fakeFootnotes

@FN[The original source book presents this as replacing the *slow fall* increase at 19th level, but the ability actually increases at 18th level. This would mean that the ability would increase to 90 feet at 18th level, then revert down to 80 feet at 19th level, which would be strange. There is no official errata confirming this, but the ability is presented above as if it was adjusted at 18th level.]{from=1}

kata_master.name: Kata Master
description: ## Kata Master
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/105›

The kata master takes the visual aspect of his martial art to its logical extreme, harnessing her flowing movements and skilled maneuvers as a psychological weapon against her enemies. A kata master forsakes the mental discipline of her more contemplative brethren in favor of these flamboyant exhibitions. She often performs in staged fights and tournaments, utilizing stylized forms to amaze the audience and shock and dismay her opponents.

::ab[Panache]{l=1 icon=power ability="A kata master gains the ‹class/swashbuckler«'s› *panache* class ability. At the start of each day, a kata master gains a number of *panache* points equal to her Charisma bonus (minimum 1). Her *panache* goes up or down throughout the day, but usually cannot go higher than his Charisma bonus (minimum 1). A kata master gains the swashbuckler's ‹swashdeed/derring-do› and ‹swashdeed/dodging panache› deeds. A kata master can use an unarmed strike or monk special weapon in place of a light or one-handed piercing melee weapon for granted swashbuckler class features and deeds." replace="Stunning fist"}
::ab[Menacing Swordplay (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def ability="A kata master gains the swashbuckler's ‹swashdeed/menacing swordplay› deed." replace="Still mind"}
::ab[Ki Pool (Su)]{l=4 icon=def ability="A kata master can treat ki points as *panache* points for any swashbuckler deed gained through this archetype." alter="Ki pool"}
::ab[Targeted Strike (Ex)]{l=7 icon=def ability="A kata master gains the swashbuckler's ‹swashdeed/targeted strike› deed." replace="Wholeness of body"}
::ab[Dizzying Defense (Ex)]{l=15 icon=def ability="A kata master gains the ‹swashdeed/dizzying defense› swashbuckler deed." replace="Quivering palm"}

ki_mystic.name: Ki Mystic
description: ## Ki Mystic
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/111›

The ki mystic believes that violence is sometimes necessary, but knowing and understanding is the true root of perfection. Through meditation and spiritual visions, a ki mystic can see beyond the veil of reality to the underlying truth of all existence. A ki mystic has the following class features.

::ab[Ki Mystic (Su)]{l=3 icon=power-boost ability="A ki mystic gains a pool of ki points equal to his Wisdom modifier. If the monk has at least 1 point of ki in his *ki pool,* he gains a +2 bonus on all Knowledge skill checks." order=aw swift="The monk can spend 1 ki point immediately before making an ability or skill check to gain a +4 insight bonus on the check." imp4="The pool increases to 1/2 his monk level + his Wisdom modifier + 2." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Mystic Insight (Su)]{l=5 icon=roll flavor="A ki mystic becomes apt at giving just the right word of advice in just the nick of time." immediate="The monk can spend 2 ki points to grant an ally within 30 feet the ability to reroll a single attack roll or saving throw. The ally must be able to hear the monk to gain the reroll benefit." replace="Purity of body"}

::ab[Mystic Visions (Su)]{l=11 icon=magic ability="A ki mystic may receive mystic visions when he rests. These visions can come as a dream, an epiphany, or even as the voice of an old friend whispering in the monk's mind. The effect is similar to a ‹spell/divination› spell with a caster level equal to the monk's level. The divination has no casting time; it is just part of the normal dreams or visions that occur every night. Using this ability costs 2 ki points that are removed from the next day's total." replace="Diamond body"}
::ab[Mystic Prescience (Su)]{icon=def l13="A ki mystic gains a +2 insight bonus to AC and CMD." l20="The bonus increases to +4." replace="Diamond soul"}

::ab[Mystic Persistence (Su)]{l=19 icon=aura-roll swift="A ki mystic can create an aura once per day at the cost of at least 2 points of ki. The aura emanates out to a 20-foot radius. The monk and all allies within the aura can roll two dice when making an attack roll or a saving throw and take the better result. The aura lasts for 1 round, plus an additional round for every 2 ki points spent when the monk created the aura." special="The monk can dismiss the aura at any time as a @HLfree_action, but the ki points for the full duration of the aura are lost." replace="Empty body"}

lifting_hand.name: Lifting Hand
description: ## Lifting Hand
‹SOURCE Martial Arts Handbook/23›

Those studied in the art of grappling know that using their opponents' own weight and momentum to lift, disable, or throw them is impressive and effective. When facing a master of the lifting hand, an enemy is likely to find itself sailing through the air or locked in a tortuous compliance hold.

::ab[Savage Toss (Ex)]{icon=power-melee-lower l1="A lifting hand gains ‹feat/Savage Slam› as a bonus feat, even if she doesn't meet the prerequisites." l4="When the lifting hand succeeds at her grapple combat maneuver check while using the Savage Slam feat and her grapple combat maneuver check result exceeds the opponent's CMD by at least 5, she can throw the opponent into an non-adjacent square. The lifting hand can throw the opponent 10 feet, plus an additional 5 feet for every 5 by which her grapple check exceeds the opponent's CMD. For instance, if the lifting monk exceeds the target's CMD by 15, she can throw the target into a square 20 feet away." l8="When using Savage Slam, the lifting hand's target no longer receives a bonus to its CMD when the lifting hand attempts to throw it into a dangerous area." replace="Stunning fist"}

::ab[Bonus Feats (Ex)]{icon=power l1="A lifting hand adds ‹feat/Whirling Hold› to her list of bonus feats." l6="She adds ‹feat/Dramatic Slam› and ‹feat/Overhead Flip›." l10="She adds ‹feat/Savage Leap›." alter="Bonus feats"}

::ab[Joint Lock (Ex)]{l=10 icon=melee-lower ability="When a lifting hand succeeds at a combat maneuver check to maintain a grapple, instead of choosing to damage, move, pin, or tie up the opponent, she can instead force one of the target's limbs into an agonizing position. The target becomes ‹misc/sickened› as long as the lifting hand maintains the grapple. When the grapple is released or broken, the target is ‹misc/fatigued› for a number of rounds equal to the lifting hand's level. This is a pain effect." replace="Bonus feat gained at 10th level"}

::ab[Counter-Throw (Sp)]{l=12 icon=def ability="A lifting hand can ‹rule/ready an action› to trigger if a creature tries to attack her. When the readied action triggers, the lifting hand can attempt a grapple combat maneuver check with a +2 bonus. If she succeeds, she grapples the creature and negates the triggering attack, and she can spend an @HLimmediate_action to use the Savage Slam feat." replace="Abundant step"}

maneuver_master.name: Maneuver Master
description: ## Maneuver Master
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/58›
The maneuver master specializes in more complicated moves than simple damage-dealing strikes.

::ab[Bonus Feat]{icon=power l1="In addition to normal monk bonus feats, a maneuver master may select any Improved combat maneuver feat (such as ‹feat/Improved Overrun›) as a bonus feat." l6="He may select any Greater combat maneuver feat (such as ‹feat/Greater Grapple›) as a bonus feat." l10="He may select any maneuver Strike feat (such as ‹feat/Tripping Strike›) as a bonus feat."}

::ab[Flurry of Maneuvers (Ex)]{icon=melee l1="As part of a full-attack action, a maneuver master can make one additional combat maneuver, regardless of whether the maneuver normally replaces a melee attack or requires a standard action. The maneuver master uses his monk level in place of his base attack bonus to determine his CMB for the bonus maneuvers, though all combat maneuver checks suffer a -2 penalty when using a flurry." l8="A maneuver master may attempt a second additional combat maneuver, with an additional -3 penalty on combat maneuver checks." l15="A maneuver master may attempt a third additional combat maneuver, with an additional -7 penalty on combat maneuver checks." special="A maneuver master loses this ability when wearing armor, using a shield, or carrying a medium or heavy load." replace="Flurry of blows"}

::ab[Maneuver Defense (Ex)]{l=3 icon=lower passive="If a maneuver master has an Improved combat maneuver feat, any creature attempting that maneuver against the maneuver master provokes an attack of opportunity, even if it would not normally do so." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Reliable Maneuver (Ex)]{l=4 icon=roll swift="A maneuver master may spend 1 point from his *ki pool* before attempting a combat maneuver. He can roll his combat maneuver check for that maneuver twice and use the better result." replace="Slow fall"}

::ab[Meditative Maneuver (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost swift="A maneuver master can add his Wisdom modifier on any combat maneuver check he makes before the beginning of his next turn. He must choose which combat maneuver check to grant the bonus to before making the combat maneuver check." replace="Purity of body"}

::ab[Sweeping Maneuver (Ex)]{l=11 icon=melee ability="A maneuver master can make two combat maneuvers as a @HLstandard_action, as long as neither maneuver requires the maneuver master to move. He may perform two identical maneuvers against two adjacent enemies, or he may perform two different combat maneuvers against the same target." replace="Diamond body"}

::ab[Whirlwind Maneuver (Ex)]{l=15 icon=melee fullround="Once per day, a maneuver master can attempt a single combat maneuver against every opponent he threatens, as long as the combat maneuver does not require movement. He makes a single combat maneuver check, and it applies to all targets." replace="Quivering palm"}

martial_artist.name: Martial Artist
description: ## Martial Artist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/59›
The martial artist pursues a mastery of pure martial arts without the monastic traditions. He is a master of form, but lacks the ability to harness his ki.
::ab[Alignment]{icon=info note="A martial artist may be of any alignment."}

::ab[Pain Points (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A martial artist's advanced knowledge of humanoid anatomy grants a +1 bonus on critical hit confirmation rolls and increases the DC of his *stunning fist* and *quivering palm* by 1." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Martial Arts Master (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="A martial artist may use his monk level to qualify for feats with a fighter level prerequisite when those feats are applied to unarmed strikes or weapons with the monk special quality." replace="Slow fall"}

::ab[Exploit Weakness (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost-def order=wa swift="A martial artist can observe a creature or object to find its weak point by making a Wisdom check and adding his monk level against a DC of 10 + the object's hardness or the target's CR. If the check succeeds, the martial artist gains a +2 bonus on attack rolls until the end of his turn, and any attacks he makes until the end of his turn ignore the creature or object's DR or hardness." ability="A martial artist may instead use this ability as a @HLswift_action to analyze the movements and expressions of one creature within 30 feet, granting a bonus on Sense Motive checks and Reflex saves and a dodge bonus to AC against that opponent equal to 1/2 his monk level until the start of his next turn." replace="Ki pool"}

::ab[Extreme Endurance (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def l5="A martial artist gains immunity to ‹misc/fatigue›." l10="He also gains immunity to ‹misc/exhaustion›." l15="He gains immunity to stunning." l20="He gains immunity to death effects and ‹rule/energy drain›." replace="Purity of body~diamond body~perfect self"}

::ab[Physical Resistance (Ex)]{l=7 icon=def passive="If a martial artist suffers any effect that causes ‹rule/ability damage›, ability drain, or temporary ability score penalties, the effect is reduced by 1 point." incrementPlain="This reduction~7~3" incrementEnd=" points." replace="Wholeness of body~timeless body~tongue of sun and moon"}
::ab[Bonus Feats]{l=12 icon=power ability="A martial artist gains an additional bonus feat, selected from those available in the monk class feature." replace="Abundant step"}

::ab[Defensive Roll (Ex)]{l=13 icon=def ability="A martial artist may use the defensive roll ‹ability/advanced rogue talent› once per day." incrementPlain="He may use~13~3" incrementDesc=it incrementEnd=" times per day." replace="Diamond soul"}

::ab[Quivering Palm]{alter="Quivering palm" icon=boost l16="A martial artist may use this ability one additional time per day." incrementPlain="He can now use~16~1" incrementDesc=this incrementEnd=" additional times per day." special="A martial artis may not have more than one *quivering palm* in effect at a time."}

::ab[Greater Defensive Roll (Ex)]{l=19 icon=def passive="A martial artist suffers no damage on a successful defensive roll, and only half damage if the Reflex saving throw fails." replace="Empty body"}

master_of_many_styles.name: Master of Many Styles
description: ## Master of Many Styles
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/59›
The master of many styles is a collector. For every move, he seeks a counter. For every style, he has a riposte. Ultimately, he seeks perfection through the fusion of styles.

::ab[Bonus Feat]{icon=power l1="A master of many styles may select a bonus ‹main/style feat› or the ‹feat/Elemental Fist› feat. He does not need to meet the prerequisites of that feat, except the Elemental Fist feat." l2="He may select another such feat." l6="A master of many styles can choose to instead another such feat, or gain a *wildcard style slot.* Whenever he enters one or more styles, he can spend his *wildcard style slots* to gain feats in those styles' feat paths (such as ‹feat/Earth Child Topple›) as long as he meets the prerequisites. Each time he changes styles, he can also change these *wildcard style slots.*" repeat="He gains another feat or *slot,* as above.~10~4" replace="Standard bonus feats"}

::ab[Fuse Style (Ex)]{icon=power-boost flavor="A master of many styles can fuse two of the styles he knows into a more perfect style." l1="The master of many styles can have two style feat stances active at once. Starting a stance provided by a style feat is still a @HLswift_action, but when the master of many styles switches to another style feat, he can choose one style whose stance is already active to persist. He may only have two style feat stances active at a time." l8="The master of many styles can fuse three styles at once, having the stances of three style feats active at the same time. He gains a bonus on attack rolls equal to the number of styles whose stances he currently has active. Furthermore, he can enter up to three stances as a @HLswift_action." l15="The master of many styles can fuse four styles at once, having the stances of four style feats active at the same time. Furthermore, he can enter up to four stances as a @HLfree_action by spending 1 point from his *ki pool.*" replace="Flurry of blows"}

::ab[Perfect Style (Ex)]{l=20 icon=power ability="A master of many styles can have the stances of five style feats active at once, and can change those stances as a @HLfree_action." replace="Perfect self"}

menhir_guardian.name: Menhir Guardian
description: ## Menhir Guardian
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/64›
Menhir guardians are ascetic protectors of sacred druidic sites, less concerned with structure and maintaining internal order than they are with preserving the natural balance of the world.

::ab[Alignment]{icon=info note="A menhir guardian can be lawful neutral, neutral good, neutral evil, or neutral. While they can work with chaotic allies, they cannot themselves be chaotic." alter="Alignment requirement"}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down ability="A menhir guardian is proficient with the ‹eq-weapon/club›, ‹eq-weapon/dagger›, ‹eq-weapon/dart›, ‹eq-weapon/quarterstaff›, ‹eq-weapon/sickle›, ‹eq-weapon/shortspear›, ‹eq-weapon/sling›, and ‹eq-weapon/spear›. A menhir guardian can use these weapons in conjunction with his *wild flurry* ability." replace="Weapon and armor proficiency"}

::ab[Claws (Ex)]{icon=melee flavor="A menhir guardian learns to draw upon natural forces to subtly change his body." ability="He gains the ‹class/shifter«'s› *shifter claws* class feature and treats his monk level as his shifter level for the purposes of this ability." replace="Unarmed strike~ki strike" alter="Ki pool"}

::ab[Wild Flurry (Ex)]{icon=melee ability="A menhir guardian gains *flurry of blows.* He can use this ability with the natural attacks provided by his *shifter claws* ability or the weapons specified above under weapon and armor proficiency." alter="Furry of blows"}

::ab[Rebuking Strike (Ex)]{l=1 icon=melee flavor="A menhir guardian can channel his ki into a devastating strike that repels his enemies." ability="A menhir guardian must declare that he is using *rebuking strike* before making a natural attack with his claws. On a hit, a foe damaged by the menhir guardian's claws must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + half the menhir guardian's level + his Wisdom modifier) or be pushed back 5 feet directly away from the menhir guardian." incrementPlain="The distance the foe is pushed~1~4~10~5" incrementEnd=" feet." special="If this movement would be blocked by a solid object, the target of *rebuking strike* takes 1d6 points of damage per 10 feet moved and falls ‹rule/prone› at the end of the movement." imp5="A creature pushed also has its speed reduced by half for a number of rounds equal to the menhir guardian's level." imp15="A pushed creature is also affected by ‹spell/aversion›, making it avoid the menhir guardian's current location for 1 round per level the menhir guardian has." usage="The menhir guardian can attempt a *rebuking strike* a number of times per day equal to his monk level, but only once per round." replace="Stunning fist~high jump~quivering palm"}

monk_of_the_empty_hand.name: Monk of the Empty Hand
description: ## Monk of the Empty Hand
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/111›

The monk of the empty hand eschews normal weapons in favor of whatever is lying around - rocks, chair legs, flagons of ale, even a simple quill pen all become deadly weapons in the hands of such a monk. A monk of the empty hand draws on his own ki to infuse his improvised weapons with power, and can transform a broken bottle into a magical weapon.

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power-down ability="Monks of the empty hand are proficient with the ‹eq-weapon/shuriken› only. A monk of the empty hand treats normal weapons as ‹misc/improvised weapons› with the following equivalencies (substituting all of their statistics for the listed weapon): a light weapon functions as a ‹eq-weapon/light hammer›, a one-handed weapon functions as a ‹eq-weapon/club›, and a two-handed weapon functions as a ‹eq-weapon/quarterstaff›." replace="Weapon proficiencies"}

::ab[Flurry of Blows (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A monk of the empty hand can make a *flurry of blows* using any combination of unarmed strikes or attacks with an improvised weapon. He may not make a *flurry of blows* with any other weapons, including special monk weapons. A monk of the empty hand's *flurry of blows* otherwise functions as normal for a monk of his level." alter="Flurry of blows"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{icon=power l6="A monk of the empty hand adds the following feats to his list of bonus feats: ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick›, ‹feat/Improved Steal›, and ‹feat/Improvised Weapon Mastery›."}

::ab[Versatile Improvisation (Ex)]{l=3 icon=melee swift="A monk of the empty hand may use an improvised weapon to deal damage as if it were another type (bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing) for 1 round, regardless of the weapon's normal damage type." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Ki Pool (Su)]{l=4 icon=ranged-boost ability="In addition to the normal abilities of his *ki pool,* a monk of the empty hand may spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to increase the range increment for an improvised thrown weapon or shuriken by 20 feet for 1 round."}

::ab[Ki Weapons (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost swift="A monk of the empty hand may spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to deal damage equal to his unarmed strike damage with an improvised weapon for 1 round." imp11="The monk may spend ki to grant an enhancement bonus or magical weapon abilities to an improvised weapon for 1 round, at the rate of 1 point of ki per +1 bonus or its equivalent. The monk may not spend more than 3 points of ki at one time for this purpose. For example, a monk can spend 2 points of ki to give his improvised weapon a +1 enhancement bonus and the ‹magic-enh/ki focus› quality, or just the ‹magic-enh/flaming burst› quality." imp15="The limit increases to 5 ki per round. The monk may use this ability to add magical weapon qualities to improvised weapons that could not normally have such a quality, such as adding the ‹magic-enh/disruption› quality to a slashing weapon, or the ‹magic-enh/vorpal› quality to a bludgeoning weapon." replace="Purity of body~diamond body"}

monk_of_the_four_winds.name: Monk of the Four Winds
description: ## Monk of the Four Winds
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/112›

The monk of the four winds is connected to the natural world in a way few other creatures - even other monks - can hope to match. He can call upon the elements and the spirits of the world in times of need, and as he nears his goal of perfection, he gains the ability to slow down time and even defeat death itself. A monk of the four winds has the following class features.

::ab[Elemental Fist (Su)]{icon=power-melee l1="A monk of the four winds gains ‹feat/Elemental Fist› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites." l5="The monk increases the damage of his Elemental Fist by 1d6." imp10="The damage is now increased by 2d6." imp15="The damage is now increased by 3d6." imp20="The damage is now increased by 4d6." replace="Stunning Fist"}

::ab[Slow Time (Su)]{l=12 icon=magic-power flavor="A monk of the four winds can use his ki to slow time or quicken his movements, depending on the observer." swift="The monk can expend 6 ki points to gain three standard actions during his turn instead of just one. The monk can use these actions to do the following: take a melee attack action, use a skill, use an extraordinary ability, or take a move action. The monk cannot use these actions to cast spells or use spell-like abilities, and cannot combine them to take full-attack actions. Any move actions the monk makes this turn do not provoke attacks of opportunity." replace="Abundant step"}

::ab[Aspect Master (Su)]{head l=17 icon=power-warning ability="A monk of the four winds must choose an aspect of one of the great spirits of the world. Once made, this choice cannot be changed. This spirit grants the monk a new appearance and new abilities, as well as changing or augmenting the monk's personality in some way. Once this choice is made, it cannot be changed." special="The monk must abide by the alignment restrictions of the aspect. If the monk ever changes his alignment to something outside the aspect's alignment restrictions, he loses this ability and cannot regain it unless his alignment later changes again to match that of the aspect." replace="Timeless body"}

::ab[Aspect of the Carp]{next icon=power ability="The monk's skin becomes a coat of golden, iridescent fish scales, his neck grows gills, and his fingers become webbed. He can breathe water and gains a swim speed equal to his land speed." special="The carp is heroic and adventurous - a monk must be nonevil to take on the aspect of the carp."}

::ab[Aspect of the Ki-Rin]{next icon=power ability="The monk's skin takes on a golden luminescence, and a silvery mane that cannot be bound grows atop his head. He gains a fly speed equal to his land speed, but he must end each turn on the ground. If the monk does not land by the end of his turn, he falls from whatever height he has attained." special="The ki-rin is honorable, honest, and self-sacrificing - a monk must be lawful good to take on the aspect of the ki-rin."}

::ab[Aspect of the Monkey]{next icon=power-melee ability="The monk's face becomes that of a monkey, and he grows a prehensile tail. The monk can pick up objects and make unarmed attacks with his tail (though the tail does not grant additional unarmed attacks or natural attacks). In addition, the monk gains a climb speed equal to his land speed." special="The monkey is a creature of whimsy and a lover of pranks - a monk of any alignment can take on the aspect of the monkey."}

::ab[Aspect of the Oni]{next icon=magic ability="The monk's skin becomes pitch black, and his hair turns white, black, red, or violet. He can assume ‹spell/gaseous form› (as the spell) as a standard action for 1 minute per day per monk level. This duration does not need to be consecutive, but it must be spent in 1-minute increments." special="The oni is treacherous and deceitful, and it hungers for the pain and death of living creatures - a monk must be evil to take on the aspect of the oni."}

::ab[Aspect of the Owl]{next icon=power ability="The monk grows feathers, and his head becomes avian, with wide, unblinking eyes. He gains a fly speed of 30 feet." special="The owl is a sage creature, deeply serious, and driven toward a single goal - a monk of any alignment can take on the aspect of the owl."}

::ab[Aspect of the Tiger]{next icon=melee ability="Dark stripes appear on the monk's skin, and his face becomes more feline. His eyes become cat-like, with vertical pupils, and his canines enlarge. Once per hour, the monk can move at 10 times his normal land speed when he makes a charge and is treated as if he had the ‹umr/pounce› ability." special="The tiger is swift, fierce, and deadly - a monk of any alignment can take on the aspect of the tiger."}

::ab[Immortality (Su)]{l=20 icon=def passive="A monk of the four winds no longer ages. He remains in his current age category forever. Even if the monk comes to a violent end, he spontaneously ‹spell/reincarnate«s› (as the spell) 24 hours later in a place of his choosing within 20 miles of the place he died. The monk must have visited the place in which he returns back to life at least once." replace="Perfect self"}

monk_of_the_healing_hand.name: Monk of the Healing Hand
description: ## Monk of the Healing Hand
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/113›

Monks of the healing hand seek perfection through helping others. By focusing their meditations on the flow of life within themselves and all creation they gain an understanding of how to share their ki with others, healing wounds and even bringing the dead back to life. For such a monk, sacrificing himself to save another is the surest way to achieve transcendence. A monk of the healing hand has the following class features.

::ab[Ancient Healing Hand (Su)]{l=7 icon=aid flavor="A monk of the healing hand can heal another creature's wounds with a touch." fullround="The monk can spend 2 ki points to heal a number of hit points equal to the monk's level. He needs at least one hand free to use this ability, and cannot heal himself. If the action is interrupted, the subject heals no hit points, and the ki points are lost." replace="Wholeness of body"}

::ab[Ki Sacrifice (Su)]{l=11 icon=magic ability="A monk of the healing hand can use his entire *ki pool* to bring a person back to life. It takes 1 hour to perform this ritual. At the end of the ritual, the monk sacrifices all of his ki in order to cast ‹spell/raise dead› (as the spell) with a caster level equal to his monk level. The ritual uses all of the ki in the monk's *ki pool;* the monk must have at least 6 points of ki in his *ki pool* to use this ability." imp15="The monk may sacrifice his ki to cast ‹spell/resurrection›. The monk must have at least 8 points of ki in his *ki pool* to use this ability." special="These rituals do not require material components. When this ability is used, the monk's *ki pool* is not replenished until 24 hours have passed." replace="Diamond body~quivering palm"}

::ab[True Sacrifice (Su)]{l=20 icon=aura-magic-down ability="In a final selfless act, a monk of the healing hand can draw in his entire ki, which then explodes outward in a 50-foot-radius ‹misc/emanation›. All dead allies within the emanation are brought back to life, as if they were the subject of a ‹spell/true resurrection› spell with a caster level equal to the monk's level." special="When the monk does this, he is truly and utterly destroyed. A monk destroyed in this way can never come back to life, not even by way of a ‹spell/wish› or ‹spell/miracle› spell or by the power of a deity. Furthermore, the monk's name can never be spoken or written down again. All written mentions of his name become nothing more than a blank space." replace="Perfect self"}

monk_of_the_lotus.name: Monk of the Lotus
description: ## Monk of the Lotus
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/114›

Monks are warriors who hone their bodies into deadly weapons, but some monks eschew violence in favor of a more peaceful philosophy. While a monk of the lotus realizes that combat cannot always be avoided - and is more than capable in a fight - he understands that all creatures are connected, and to harm another is to harm the self. Instead, he strives to find peaceful resolutions to conflicts, and in doing so, hopes to achieve inner peace. A monk of the lotus has the following class features.

::ab[Touch of Serenity (Su)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A monk of the lotus gains ‹feat/Touch of Serenity› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites. The duration of Touch of Serenity is increased by ‹misc/one-sixth› of his monk level." special="Each round on its turn, the target may attempt a new Will save to end the effect. This duration does not stack; only the longest remaining duration applies." replace="Stunning Fist"}

::ab[Touch of Surrender (Su)]{l=12 icon=melee-lower flavor="A monk of the lotus makes a foe into a friend with a single show of mercy." immediate="When one of his melee attacks would reduce a creature to 0 or fewer hit points, the monk can spend 6 ki points to make the target of that attack surrender. When the target surrenders, it is reduced to 0 hit points, becomes ‹misc/disabled›, and is charmed, as if the monk had cast ‹spell/charm monster› with a caster level equal to the monk's level. The target does not get a saving throw against this effect." special="This charm lasts until its duration expires, until the monk dismisses it or uses it on another creature, or until the target is again reduced to 0 or fewer hit points, whichever happens first. The monk can only have one creature charmed with *touch of surrender* at a time. This is a mind-affecting charm effect." replace="Abundant step"}

::ab[Touch of Peace (Su)]{l=15 icon=melee flavor="A monk of the lotus can set up vibrations within the body of another creature to win over the creature's mind." ability="The monk can use *touch of peace* once per day, and must announce his intent before making his attack roll. On a successful hit, the attack deals no damage, but the target is charmed as if the monk had cast *charm monster* with a caster level equal to the monk's level. The target does not get a saving throw against this effect. The creature is charmed for 1 day per level." special="If the monk or his allies attack the charmed creature, or if the monk asks or commands the charmed creature to take hostile actions, the effect ends. This is a mind-affecting charm effect." replace="Quivering palm"}

::ab[Learned Master (Ex)]{l=17 icon=boost ability="A monk of the lotus gains all ‹skill/Knowledge› skills and the ‹skill/Linguistics› skill as class skills. The monk uses Wisdom instead of Intelligence as the key ability for these skills." replace="Tongue of the sun and the moon"}

monk_of_the_mantis.name: Monk of the Mantis
description: ## Monk of the Mantis
‹SOURCE Dirty Tactics Toolbox/11›

A body contains many points where the flesh, mind, and spirit coincide. A monk of the mantis is skilled at manipulating these points. With precise strikes, she temporarily disrupts a body's connection with the rest of the self.

::ab[Pressuring Strikes (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power ability="A monk of the mantis gains the ‹ability/sneak attack› ability. This ability functions as the ‹class/rogue› ability of the same name, except it can be used only during a *flurry of blows.* The initial sneak attack damage is +1d6." increment="The damage~2~4" incrementEnd="d6." replace="2nd-, 6th-, 10th-, 14th-, and 18th-level bonus feats"}

::ab[Debilitating Blows (Ex)]{flavor="A monk of the mantis's knowledge of anatomy allows her to deal blows that both deal damage and impose debilitating conditions." icon=lower l7="If a monk of the mantis stuns a target with ‹feat/Stunning Fist›, the target is also ‹rule/entangled› for 1 round after the Stunning Fist effect ends." l11="If the monk of the mantis stuns a target with Stunning Fist, the target is also ‹misc/exhausted› for 1 round after the Stunning Fist effect ends." l13="The monk can spend 1 point from her *ki pool* as part of her Stunning Fist attack to increase the duration of these additional effects to a number of rounds equal to her Wisdom bonus (minimum 2 rounds)." replace="Wholeness of body~diamond body~diamond soul"}

::ab[Disabling Palm (Su)]{l=15 icon=magic ability="A monk of the mantis can render the target of her *quivering palm* unconscious instead of killing the creature. Upon failing its Fortitude saving throw, the target either is knocked ‹misc/unconscious› for a number of minutes equal to the monk of the mantis's monk level or dies (the monk of the mantis's choice)." alter="Quivering palm"}

monk_of_the_mantis_uc.name: Monk of the Mantis (Unchained)
regex: special="This ability only applies if the monk selects the *quivering palm* ki power."

monk_of_the_sacred_mountain.name: Monk of the Sacred Mountain
description: ## Monk of the Sacred Mountain
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/114›

The monk of the sacred mountain finds strength and power in the earth beneath his feet. Rather than spinning though the battlefield with the fluid motion of the river, he roots himself to the ground, as immovable and unshakable as the stones of the mountain. A monk of the sacred mountain has the following class features.
::ab[Iron Monk (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-def ability="A monk of the sacred mountain gains ‹feat/Toughness› as a bonus feat." passive="The monk gains a +1 natural armor bonus." order=ap replace=Evasion}

::ab[Bastion Stance (Ex)]{icon=def l4="A monk of the sacred mountain becomes like stone, nearly impossible to move when he stands his ground. If the monk starts and ends his turn in the same space, he cannot be knocked ‹rule/prone› or forcibly moved until the start of his next turn, except by mind-affecting or teleportation effects." l16="He is immune to any attempts to force him to move, even mind-affecting and teleportation effects." replace="Slow fall"}

::ab[Iron Limb Defense (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def-boost ability="A monk of the sacred mountain can deflect blows with an active defense that complements his bastion stance. If the monk starts and ends his turn in the same space, he gains a +2 shield bonus to AC and CMD until the start of his next turn." swift="He can spend 1 ki point to increase this bonus to +4." order=aw replace="High jump"}

::ab[Adamantine Monk (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="A monk of the sacred mountain has muscles so strong and skin so resilient that he gains DR 1/-." order=pw incrementPlain="The monk's DR~9~3" incrementEnd="/-." swift="The monk can spend 1 ki point to double his DR until the beginning of his next turn." replace="Improved evasion"}

::ab[Vow of Silence (Su)]{l=17 icon=def-boost-warning passive="A monk of the sacred mountain becomes as impassive as stone, making a vow of silence in exchange for greater abilities. The monk gains a +2 insight bonus to AC and CMD and a +4 bonus on Sense Motive, Stealth, and Perception checks." special="The monk does not lose the capacity for speech, but if he ever speaks, he loses this feature for 24 hours." replace="Tongue of the sun and the moon"}

monk_of_the_seven_forms.name: Monk of the Seven Forms
description: ## Monk of the Seven Forms
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/40›

When Tian monks founded the Monastery of the Seven Forms near the present border between Qadira and Taldor, they did so as a challenge to prospective students to travel great distances and prove their devotion before training within the monastery's walls. Over the centuries, the monastery has fallen under Qadiran control many times, and the original seven forms for which the monastery was named have been lost to history. In their place, the monks of the isolated sanctuary now teach a style featuring lightning-fast strikes that mimic both dervish dances and the searing winds of the arid desert, favoring mobility and endurance.

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=down-power l1="A monk of the seven forms loses the ability to take ‹feat/Improved Disarm›, ‹feat/Improved Feint›, ‹feat/Improved Grapple›, and ‹feat/Improved Trip› as bonus feats. He adds ‹feat/Nimble Moves› to his list of bonus feats." l6="He also adds ‹feat/Acrobatic Steps› and ‹feat/Wind Stance› to his list of bonus feats." l10="He adds ‹feat/Lightning Stance› to his list of bonus feats."}

::ab[Lightning Finish (Ex)]{l=1 icon=melee immediate="A monk of the seven forms can make a single attack with a manufactured light or one-handed slashing weapon he is currently wielding against any target he has successfully hit at least twice with his unarmed strike during his turn. This attack deals normal damage but without the normal Strength bonus to damage." imp4="He can take a 5-foot step before or after performing a *lightning finish* so long as he ends adjacent to his target, though if he takes this step, he cannot perform a 5-foot step during his next turn." imp8="He applies his full Strength bonus to his damage roll for the extra attack, even if he made the attack with an off-hand weapon or with a weapon wielded in two hands." imp12="If he hit the target at least three times with his unarmed strike during his turn, taking the 5-foot step granted by *lightning finish* does not prevent him from taking a 5-foot step during his next turn." usage="A monk of the seven forms can use this ability a number of times per day equal to his monk level, but no more than once per round." replace="Stunning fist"}
::ab[Endurance (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A monk of the seven forms gains ‹feat/Endurance› as a bonus feat." replace="Maneuver training"}

::ab[Sirocco Fury (Su)]{l=11 icon=melee fullround="A monk of the seven forms can spend 2 ki points to enter a meditative trance that replicates the spell ‹spell/dance of a hundred cuts›, using his monk level as his caster level. Unlike the spell, a monk of the seven forms must both move 10 feet and make a melee attack each round after he begins this trance to prevent the effect from ending." imp15="He can instead spend 3 ki points when using this ability to make his trance replicate ‹spell/dance of a thousand cuts› with the same limitations." replace="Abundant step~quivering palm"}

nimble_guardian.name: Nimble Guardian
description: ## Nimble Guardian
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/92›

Some catfolk monks dedicate their graceful prowess to the defense of others, especially those dedicated to a similar ethos or who prove themselves as stalwart allies of the monk's cause. A nimble guardian has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Catfolk}

::ab[Defensive Aid (Ex)]{l=2 icon=protect useMod=Wisdom flavor="A nimble guardian can interpose herself between one adjacent ally and an attack or damage dealt in an area of effect." immediate="If an adjacent ally is the target of the attack or is required to make a Reflex saving throw against a damaging effect, the nimble guardian can grant that ally a +4 circumstance bonus to AC or on the saving throw against the effect." special="The nimble guardian must use this ability before the attack roll or saving throw is made. The nimble guardian can only use this ability if he is wearing light or no armor." replace=Evasion}
::ab[Nimble Reflexes (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A nimble guardian gains a +2 bonus on all Reflex saving throws." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Defensive Mastery (Ex)]{l=5 icon=protect ability="A nimble guardian gains 3 additional uses of her *defensive aid* ability per day. Furthermore, if an ally that gained the benefit of a use of defensive aid succeeds her Reflex saving throw, and the effect still deals damage on a successful saving throw, the nimble guardian can spend 1 ki point to negate that damage. Doing so is not an action." replace="Purity of body"}

::ab[Guardian Feline (Su)]{l=7 icon=magic provokes=No standard="A nimble guardian can transform himself into a feline creature by spending 2 ki points. The effect lasts for 1 hour or until the nimble guardian changes back. The chosen form must be some form of feline (‹monster/cheetah›, ‹monster/lion›, etc.). This ability is otherwise identical to ‹spell/beast shape II›." imp9="This ability functions as ‹spell/beast shape III›." replace="Wholeness of body"}
::ab[Evasion (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def ability="A nimble guardian gains ‹ability/evasion›." replace="Improved evasion"}

nornkith.name: Nornkith
description: ## Nornkith
‹SOURCE Legacy of the First World/24›

Nornkith hone their bodies and minds to follow the branching threefold paths of fate. Nornkith seek to emulate the fey masters of fate called ‹monster/norn«s› and eventually ascend to become norns in their own right.
::ab[Conviction of Fate (Ex)]{icon=info note="A nornkith uses her Charisma, rather than her Wisdom, to determine all class features and effects relating to his monk class."}

::ab[Forewarned (Su)]{icon=power-boost-roll ability="A nornkith can always act in the surprise round, even if he does not notice her enemies, though she remains ‹rule/flat-footed› until she takes an action. In addition, she gains a bonus on initiative rolls equal to half her monk level (minimum +1)." imp20="A nornkith's initiative roll is automatically a natural 20." replace="Stunning fist"}

::ab[Shears (Su)]{icon=melee-power l1="Whenever a nornkith makes an unarmed strike, she can cause shimmering silver shears to manifest around the blow, causing the unarmed strike to count as silver for the purpose of overcoming ‹rule/damage reduction›." l7="She gains ‹feat/Improved Critical› (unarmed strike) as a bonus feat, even if she does not meet the prerequisites." replace="Bonus feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Resist Death's Lure (Su)]{l=5 icon=def passive="A nornkith gains a +4 morale bonus on saves against death effects, ‹rule/energy drain›, and negative energy." imp10="She gains immunity to energy drain and negative energy." replace="Purity of body~bonus feat gained at 10th level"}

::ab[Fate Weaver (Su)]{l=11 icon=roll immediate="A nornkith can spend 1 ki point to force a creature within 60 feet to reroll one saving throw before the results of the roll are revealed. The creature must take the result of the second roll, even if it's worse than the original roll. Whether the new result is a success or failure, the creature cannot be the target of this ability again for 1 day." replace="Diamond soul"}

::ab[Snip Thread (Su)]{l=15 icon=zap standard="A monk can spend 1 ki point to produce a golden thread linked to the fate of a creature within 60 feet and mimic snipping through the thread with a pair of shears. The target takes 1d6 points of damage per nornkith level and can attempt a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + half the nornkith's monk level + her Charisma modifier) to halve this damage. If the damage would bring the target's hit points to 0 or below, the nornkith can spend another ki point to sever the golden thread completely; in this case, the target dies and can only be restored to life with ‹spell/miracle›, ‹spell/wish›, or divine intervention." usage="1 time/day" special="This is a death effect." replace="Quivering palm"}

::ab[Inscrutable (Su)]{l=19 icon=def passive="The nornkith is protected by a constant ‹spell/mind blank› effect with a caster level equal to her monk level. The nornkith can suppress or resume this protection as a @HLstandard_action." replace="Empty body"}
::ab[Norn Transformation]{l=20 icon=power-def ability="A nornkith attains her fey heritage. Her type changes to ‹type/fey›, and she gains DR 10/cold iron." replace="Perfect self"}

ouat.name: Ouat
description: ## Ouat
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Combat/41›

The ascetic, desert-dwelling dwarves known as the Ouat have eschewed dwarven traditions, whose unchanging rigidity, they believe, shackles their people to the past and inhibits self-improvement. Shaving their heads, living under the open sky, and practicing arts unfamiliar to the typical ‹race/dwarf›, the Ouat follow in the footsteps of Irori and Nethys by aspiring to awaken the divine essence that lives within each creature.
::prereq{other="‹race/Dwarf› with the *greed, hatred, defensive training, stonecunning,* and *weapon familiarity* racial traits."}

::ab[Awaken Divinity (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-protect-roll l1="As a @HLstandard_action, an Ouat can touch a creature to grant it a temporary ki point until the beginning of the Ouat's next turn. In addition to any other ways in which the target can use ki, it can expend the ki point as a @HLswift_action in order to either gain a +2 dodge bonus to AC for 1 round or increase its base speed by 20 feet for 1 round. The Ouat can target himself with this ability as a @HLswift_action, but doing so costs 2 uses of the ability." l4="The dodge bonus to AC increases to +4." l8="An affected creature can spend the ki point to ignore all Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution penalties it would otherwise take for 1 round." l12="An Ouat can target two adjacent creatures with this ability as a @HLstandard_action, but must expend one use of the ability for each target affected." l16="An affected creature can spend this ki point to roll all saving throws twice and take the higher result for 1 round." l20="An affected creature can spend the ki point to benefit from the Ouat's *perfect self* class ability for 1 round. If the creature already has the *perfect self* class feature, its damage reduction and any spell resistance it has increases by 2 for 1 round." useL=monk useUnit=time special="An Ouat can use this ability no more than once per round." replace="Stunning fist~and the **greed** and **hatred** dwarf racial traits"}

::ab[Spurn Tradition (Ex)]{flavor="Ouat dwarves strive to separate themselves from the rest of their race and the traditions that bind them." icon=def-power note="The bonuses for nonmagical effects that target or specifically affect dwarf subtype creatures (such as a ranger's ‹ability/favored enemy› class feature) are all reduced by half. In addition, an Ouat becomes proficient in one martial weapon with the monk property." replace="The **defensive training, stonecunning,** and **weapon familiarity** dwarf racial traits"}

::ab[Know the Unseen Disciples (Su)]{l=7 icon=power standard="An Ouat can spot hidden creatures, as if using the spell ‹spell/see invisibility›. Using this ability consumes 2 points from his *ki pool.* His caster level for this effect is equal to his class level." replace="Wholeness of body"}

perfect_scholar.name: Perfect Scholar
description: ## Perfect Scholar
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Intrigue/44›

Perfect scholars, often worshipers of Irori, hone their minds and bodies through the accumulation of knowledge. They study and annotate the holy texts of Irori and other philosophies as well as collect lore on anatomy, medicine, philosophy, martial arts, and history, among other topics of erudition.
::cskill{gain=ka~kd~ke~kg~kh~kl~kna~kno~kp~kr~ling lose=intm~perf}
::ab[Lore (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="The perfect scholar gains a bonus equal to 1/2 his monk level on Knowledge checks and can attempt Knowledge checks untrained." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Learn from Failure (Ex)]{l=4 icon=boost ability="When the perfect scholar misses with an attack roll or fails a ‹rule/Research« check›, he gains an insight bonus on his next attack roll or Research check against the same target attempted in the next 24 hours. The insight bonus is equal to ‹misc/half› his monk level - 1." special="An individual target cannot be affected by this ability more than once in a 24-hour period." replace="Slow fall"}
::ab[Eye of the Sun and Moon (Ex)]{l=17 icon=power ability="A perfect scholar can read all languages and write all languages he has read or heard." replace="Tongue of the sun and moon"}

::ab[Walk with the Master (Su)]{l=20 icon=magic ability="The perfect scholar has mastered his spiritual resonance, allowing him to travel as if using ‹spell/etherealness›, ‹spell/plane shift›, or ‹spell/shadow walk› at his monk level by spending only 1 point from his *ki pool.* He cannot bring other creatures with him. He is treated as an ‹type/outsider› rather than his original type, although he can be brought back to life as if he were still of his former type." alter="Perfect self"}

qinggong_monk.name: Qinggong Monk
description: ## Qinggong Monk
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/51;Martial Arts Handbook/21;Advanced Race Guide/149›

The qinggong monk is a master of her ki, using it to perform superhuman stunts or even blast opponents with supernatural energy. Some achieve their power over ki through extreme discipline, while others attain this power by intentionally or accidentally ingesting rare herbs or strange mystical fruits, and a few are gifted these abilities by a dying qinggong master.

::ab[Ki Power]{icon=power clear head ability="A qinggong monk can select a ki power (see below) for which she qualifies in place of the following monk class abilities." imp4="Slow fall" imp5="High jump" imp7="Wholeness of body" imp11="Diamond body" imp12="Abundant step" imp13="Diamond soul" imp15="Quivering palm" imp17="Timeless body; Tongue of the sun and moon" imp19="Empty body" imp20="Perfect self" replace="The monk class abilities the qinggong monk gives up for a ki power." special="Ki powers are abilities that draw on the power of a monk's ki. The standard monk has several abilities that count as ki powers, such as *wholeness of body, abundant step,* and *empty body.* A qinggong monk can learn additional ki powers, which often replaces a non-ki monk ability such as *purity of body.* Ki powers are divided into three categories: **feats, monk abilities,** and **spells.**"}

::ab[Feats]{sub icon=power note="These ki powers duplicate the effects of specific feats. A monk does not need to qualify for a feat to select it as a ki power. For example, a qinggong monk can select Spring Attack as a ki power even if she doesn't meet the prerequisites for selecting Spring Attack as a feat. Activating one of these ki powers is a @HLfree_action on the monk's turn; until the start of her next turn, the monk is treated as if she had that feat. Some of these ki powers that duplicate feats may also be activated as an @HLimmediate_action; these powers are noted in the ki powers list."}

::ab[Monk Abilities]{sub icon=power note="Some ki powers are standard monk abilities. Even if a qinggong monk selects a different ki power in place of a standard monk ability, she can select that monk ability later as one of her ki powers."}

::ab[Spells]{sub icon=magic note="These ki powers duplicate the effects of a spell, and are spell-like abilities. A qinggong monk's class level is the caster level for these spell-like abilities, and she uses Wisdom to determine her ‹rule/concentration› check bonus."}
:::ab{title="List of Ki Powers" action=Ability icon=info}
All ki powers have a minimum monk level requirement to select them. A monk who does not meet this requirement cannot select that ki power.

Most ki powers require the monk to spend ki points; the exact amount is listed after the ki power. Ki powers that cost 0 ki do not require the monk to have any ki points in her *ki pool* to use the ability.
The saving throw against a monk's ki power, if any, is equal to 10 + 1/2 the monk's level + the monk's Wisdom bonus.
