sage_counselor.name: Sage Counselor
description: ## Sage Counselor
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/67›

Sage counselors are ascetics and mystics who leave the confines of the monastery walls to advise secular people about spiritual truths and to seek knowledge of the outside world. They often find work as mentors teaching religion and martial arts, and some of them even become counselors for people in high places. Sage counselors often speak in metaphors, knowing that indirect persuasion is more effective than speaking plainly, and they use indirect tactics in combat as well.
::cskill{gain=bluff~diplo~kno lose=climb~ea~per}

::ab[Cunning Fist (Ex)]{icon=power ability="A sage counselor gains ‹feat/Combat Expertise› as a bonus feat, and he can ignore the Intelligence prerequisite on feats that have Combat Expertise as a prerequisite." imp2="He gains ‹feat/Improved Feint›." imp6="He gains ‹feat/Greater Feint›." special="He doesn't need to meet the prerequisites of these bonus feats." replace="Bonus feats gained at 1st, 2nd, and 6th levels"}

::ab[Feinting Flurry]{l=4 icon=power-down swift="A sage counselor can spend 1 ki point while performing a *flurry of blows* to ‹skill/feint› an opponent, but he can't spend 1 ki point to make an additional attack when making a *flurry of blows.*" imp10="He can also choose to replace his first attack during a *flurry of blows* with a feint check." alter="Flurry of blows~ki pool" replace="The ability to spend ki to make an additional attack with a *flurry of blows*~bonus feat gained at 10th level"}

::ab[Deceptive Ki (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost swift="The sage counselor can spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to give himself a +4 insight bonus on his next Bluff check." replace="The ability to spend ki to increase his speed by 20 feet for 1 round" alter="Ki pool"}

sage_counselor_uc.name: Sage Counselor (Unchained)
regex: The ability to spend ki to increase his speed by 20 feet for 1 round

scaled_fist.name: Scaled Fist
description: ## Scaled Fist
‹SOURCE Legacy of Dragons/14›

Steeped in traditions that trace their origins to the warrior-monks who trained under the tutelage of draconic masters in Tian Xia, scaled fists eschew passive introspection in favor of unshakable confidence. Scaled fists learn to combine brutal intimidation with the brazen ferocity of an ancient wyrm to devastate their foes.

::ab[Draconic Might]{icon=info note="Any of the scaled fist's class abilities that make calculations based on her Wisdom (including bonus feats with DCs or uses per day, such as ‹feat/Stunning Fist›, but not Wisdom-based skills or Will saving throws) are instead based on her Charisma."}

::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power l1="A scaled fist adds ‹feat/Dragon Style› and ‹feat/Intimidating Prowess› to her list of bonus feats." l6="She adds ‹feat/Dazzling Display› and ‹feat/Dragon Ferocity›." l10="She adds ‹feat/Disheartening Display›, ‹feat/Dragon Roar› and ‹feat/Shatter Defenses›." alter="Bonus feats"}
::ab[Draconic Mettle (Su)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A scaled fist gains a +2 bonus on saving throws attempted against all fear, paralysis, and sleep effects." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Draconic Fury (Su)]{l=3 icon=melee choice="The scaled fist has gained some control over the draconic energies she studies. She must select one type of energy: acid, cold, electricity, or fire. Once this choice is made, it cannot be changed." order=cw swift="The scaled fist can expend 1 point from her *ki pool* to imbue her natural attacks with this energy, causing them to deal an extra 1d6 points of damage of the chosen energy type for a number of rounds equal to 1/2 her monk level." replace="Maneuver training"}

::ab[Draconic Breath (Su)]{l=15 icon=cone standard="A scaled fist can spend 3 points from her *ki pool* to make a breath weapon attack that deals 1d6 points of damage of her energy type per monk level in a 30-foot cone. Those caught in the area of the breath can attempt a Reflex save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the scaled fist's monk level + her Charisma modifier) to halve the normal damage." replace="Quivering palm"}

scaled_fist_uc.name: Scaled Fist (Unchained)
regex: \bl=3.*?has gained some control over the draconic energies.*?equal to 1/2 her monk level
l=4 icon=def passive="The scaled fist gains the ‹kipower/elemental fury› ki power

scarred_monk.name: Scarred Monk
description: ## Scarred Monk
‹SOURCE Horror Realms/12›

Scarred monks find unique clarity in physical discomfort. For monks who seek the truth hidden in pain, the practice of mortifications of the flesh helps to achieve enlightenment and acquire disturbing powers. Those who worship ‹faith/Zon-Kuthon› tend to be particularly eager to explore the limitations of the flesh, yet monks of his faith are not the only ones to use self-mutilation and scarification to seek personal enhancement and power.

::ab[Mortifications (Su)]{head icon=power alter="Ki pool" replace="High jump~wholeness of body~abundant step~empty body~the ability to spend ki points to do anything other than what's described below" imp4="A scarred monk chooses her first mortification, selecting one from the list of potential mortifications detailed below. When a scarred monk gains her first mortification, she gains a number of ki points equal to 1/2 her monk level + her Wisdom modifier. These ki points can only be spent to activate powers granted from mortifications or to make *ki strikes.*" incrementOrd="She selects~4~3" incrementDesc=her incrementEnd=" mortification." special="Once a scarred monk selects a mortification, she cannot change it. Some mortifications have prerequisites, such as already knowing certain mortifications or attaining a specific level (as noted in their descriptions). Unless otherwise stated, a scarred monk cannot select an individual mortification more than once." ability="Detailed below are a number of unusual, grisly, and harrowing processes known as mortifications that a monk can inflict upon herself in exchange for an unusual ki power. The monk must adhere to a philosophy that finds power in self-mortification; among the deities of the Inner Sea region, Zon-Kuthon's devoted are the most associated with this practice, but other lawful faiths, such as those dedicated to the empyreal lords ‹faith/Neshen› and ‹faith/Vildeis›, the infernal duke ‹faith/Zaebos›, or the whore queen ‹faith/Doloras›, count mortifications among their approved methods of self-enlightenment. While mortal followers of the mysterious ‹family/kyton› demagogues are rare in the Inner Sea region, the kytons also encourage their monk worshipers to invest deeply in mortifications."}

::ab[Armor of Scars (Su)]{next icon=def ability="A scarred monk can force ki into her extensive scars, hardening them like armor. Provided she has at least 1 ki point in her pool, she gains a +1 bonus to her natural armor. A scarred monk can take this mortification multiple times; its effects stack."}

::ab[Blood Eagle (Su)]{next icon=melee-boost order=nw note="The monk's rib bones are unusually prominent and press hideously against the flesh of her chest." swift="She can spend 1 ki point to cause her ribs to break through her flesh, lacerating and tearing any opponent that has engaged her in a grapple attack or that has ‹umr/swallow«ed her» whole›. This does not cause the monk any damage; a few seconds after she extrudes them, the ribs retract into her body, leaving behind angry red welts. The ribs deal an amount of slashing damage equal to her base unarmed strike damage to any creature that is currently grappling her or has swallowed her whole. If the monk attempts to escape from that grapple in the same round, she adds the amount of damage her ribs inflicted to her Escape Artist or CMB check as a circumstance bonus."}

::ab[Contortionist (Su)]{next icon=boost order=nf note="Over the years, the scarred monk has broken many of her bones and reset them at odd and repulsive angles to allow her to contort into entirely unnatural forms." free="The monk can spend 1 ki point to gain a +20 circumstance bonus on any Escape Artist check."}

::ab[Eyes Stitched Shut (Su)]{next icon=down-power ability="The scarred monk can stitch her eyes shut. This takes 1 minute to accomplish, after which the monk is effectively ‹misc/blind›. However, as long as the monk retains at least 1 point in her *ki pool,* she gains ‹umr/blindsense› to a distance of 60 feet. She can detect living creatures with at least 1 point of damage within 30 feet of her as if via ‹umr/blindsight›." order=ar fullround="The monk can remove the stitches to restore her normal sight and end this effect."}

::ab[Face Collector (Su)]{next icon=boost-def order=ns note="This mortification requires a monk to surgically remove the flesh from her face, surrendering her appearance and all associated identity. The flesh of her face heals into a featureless mass of scars at the end of this surgical ritual." standard="At the cost of 1 ki point, the scarred monk can alter her facial features to duplicate those of a dead body she touches. The body must be of the same creature type as the scarred monk, and as her face changes, the corpse's face withers away into rot and decay. This grants the scarred monk a +8 circumstance bonus on Disguise checks to appear as that individual (although the penalties for gender, race, age, and size still apply). As a side effect to this unnatural shifting of appearance, a scarred monk gains a +2 bonus on all saving throws against scrying effects. For 24 hours after claiming a new face, this bonus increases to +4."}

::ab[Doll Face (Su)]{next icon=boost prereq="*Face collector* mortification" ability="When acquiring a new face with the *face collector* mortification, the scarred monk can spend an additional 2 ki points to perform an eerie blood ritual that gives the stolen flesh the appearance of aged ceramic. When the face is stitched to her flesh, the monk gains the appearance of an aged porcelain doll, complete with cracked and peeling paint and a glassy, unblinking stare. A scarred monk with a *doll face* does not gain the bonus on Disguise checks but does gain a +4 bonus on Intimidate checks." ability2="Once per hour, the scarred monk can cause the *doll face* to shift and appear as the face of a lost loved one or friend of a single viewer within 30 feet - that targeted individual must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the scarred monk's monk level + the scarred monk's Charisma modifier) or become ‹misc/nauseated› with fear for a number of rounds equal to the number of mortifications the scarred monk has. This is a mind-affecting fear effect." special="Unlike a face of flesh, a *doll face* ages and cracks, falling off and restoring the scarred monk's true and deformed face after 24 hours." order="a2"}

::ab[Pain Binding (Su)]{next icon=melee standard="By spending 1 ki point when she makes a single unarmed strike against a living creature, the scarred monk can link her own pain to the creature struck. For a number of rounds equal to the total number of mortifications the scarred monk has, any time the scarred monk takes lethal damage, the creature she has bound her pain to must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the monk's level + the monk's Charisma modifier) or take an equal amount of nonlethal damage." special="The effects of pain binding end immediately once the pain-bound creature falls unconscious."}

::ab[Rings of Pain (Su)]{next icon=aura prereq="10th-level monk" note="The scarred monk has pierced her flesh with numerous large metal rings." order=ns standard="At the cost of 2 ki points, she can tear one of these piercings free from her flesh to flood her body with searing pain. As she does so, she exudes waves of agony in a 30-foot radius to deal damage equal to twice the monk's unarmed strike modified by twice her Wisdom modifier to all creatures in the area of effect." special="A successful Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the monk's level + her Wisdom modifier) halves the damage, but on a failed Will save, a creature is ‹misc/sickened› by the pain for 1 round."}

::ab[Share Pain (Su)]{next icon=def immediate="Whenever the monk takes damage from a weapon, she can spend 1 ki point to cause the body of her attacker to suffer the pain of that blow. The attacking creature must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 the monk's level + the monk's Wisdom modifier) or take damage equal to the amount the weapon blow inflicted on the monk and become ‹misc/staggered› from the pain for 1 round as well." special="A successful saving throw halves the damage the attacker suffers from shared pain and negates the staggered effect."}

::ab[Torturous Vision (Su)]{next prereq="*Share pain* mortification, 13th-level monk" icon=zap move="The scarred monk can spend 1 ki point to cause a creature that recently wounded her to suffer - in one sudden blast of agony - the pain of all the wounds the scarred monk has endured. The monk can target any living creature within 60 feet who has been affected by *share pain* in the last 24 hours. The creature becomes ‹misc/stunned› with pain for 1 round and then staggered for 1d3 rounds unless it succeeds at a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the monk's level + the monk's Wisdom modifier)."}

::ab[Third Eye (Su)]{next icon=magic order=na note="The scarred monk bores a hole in her brow and then implants a mummified or alchemically preserved third eye in this opening. The third eye must come from a creature of the same size and creature type as the scarred monk." ability="The scarred monk can spend 1 ki point to gain ‹spell/true seeing›, as per the spell, for 1 round." prereq="13th-level monk"}

::ab[Tongueless Master (Su)]{next icon=down-lower note="The monk assumes a vow of silence and slices out her own tongue. Thereafter, she wears the preserved appendage on a cord about her neck. She can no longer speak or cast spells with verbal components." order=na ability="Whenever she hits a creature with an unarmed strike, she can spend 1 point from her *ki pool* to steal the voice from her target. The target can resist this effect with a successful Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the scarred monk's level + her Wisdom modifier), otherwise the victim loses the ability to speak or cast spells with verbal components for a number of minutes equal to the total number of mortifications known by the scarred monk. During this time, the scarred monk can speak in the victim's voice."}

sensei.name: Sensei
description: ## Sensei
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/60›

The sensei is a revered teacher who imparts lessons on the oneness of mind, body, and spirit, along with occasional correction that is subtle and swift. Rather than using wisdom for his own benefit alone, he uses it to better those around him.
::cskill{gain=diplo~ling~ka~kd~ke~kg~kh~kl~kna~kno~kp~kr}

::ab[Advice (Ex)]{icon=power-boost ability="A sensei's *advice* is identical to ‹ability/bardic performance› (using oratory)." imp1="He can ‹performance/inspire courage›." imp3="He can ‹performance/inspire competence›." imp9="He can ‹performance/inspire greatness›." special="These abilities are equal to a bard of the sensei's level, and are usable a total number of rounds per day equal to his level + his Wisdom modifier (minimum 1)." replace="Flurry of blows~fast movement~improved evasion"}

::ab[Insightful Strike (Ex)]{l=2 icon=boost ability="A sensei may use his Wisdom bonus in lieu of his Strength or Dexterity on attack rolls and combat maneuver checks with unarmed strikes or monk weapons." replace="Evasion~bonus feat gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Mystic Wisdom (Su)]{icon=boost-aura l6="A sensei may use his *advice* ability when spending points from his *ki pool* to activate a class ability (using the normal actions required for each) in order to have that ability affect one ally within 30 feet rather than the sensei himself." l10="A sensei may affect all allies within 30 feet rather than himself (spending points from his *ki pool* only once, not once for each target). Or, a sensei may instead spend 1 point from his *ki pool* (as a @HLswift_action) while using *advice* to provide a single ally within 30 feet with *evasion, fast movement, high jump, purity of body,* or *slow fall.*" imp14="A sensei may spend 2 points to grant one of the abilities listed above to all allies within 30 feet, or *diamond body, diamond soul,* or *improved evasion* to a single ally within 30 feet." special="These abilities function at the sensei's level and last 1 round." replace="Bonus feats gained at 6th, 10th, and 14th level"}

serpent_fire_adept.name: Serpent-Fire Adept
description: ## Serpent-Fire Adept
‹SOURCE Occult Origins/20›
A serpent-fire adept embraces the secrets of her chakras and mastery of their energetic flow.
::cskill{gain=app~diplo~fly~heal~ka~ling~sur lose=climb~intm~kh~perf~ride~swim}

::ab[Chakra Training]{icon=power ability="A serpent-fire adept gains ‹feat/Chakra Initiate› and ‹feat/Psychic Sensitivity› as bonus feats. She need not meet these feats' prerequisites, and can use her serpent-fire ki from Chakra Initiate to open and maintain her root chakra, despite not being 2nd level." replace="Stunning fist~bonus feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Chakra Expertise (Ex)]{icon=def l2="The serpent-fire adept gains a bonus equal to 1/2 her monk level on Fortitude and Will saves attempted to maintain awakened chakras." l8="When she has opened one or more chakras, she can maintain those chakras (without opening any new chakras) for 1 round without taking a swift action, expending ki, or attempting Fortitude or Will saves to maintain the chakras." l16="This increases to 2 rounds." special="After maintaining her chakras with *chakra expertise,* the monk must open a new chakra or maintain her open chakras normally before she can use *chakra expertise* again." replace="Bonus feat gained at 2nd level"}

::ab[Light Spirit (Su)]{l=4 icon=power-boost ability="When the serpent-fire adept has her sacral chakra open and chooses to benefit from its power, her fly speed is equal to either her base land speed or her bonus to her base speed from the monk *fast movement* class ability (whichever is higher). She maintains this fly speed for a number of rounds equal to her Wisdom bonus (minimum 1 round), even if she closes her chakras." replace="Slow fall~high jump"}

::ab[Chakra Adept (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power ability="The serpent-fire adept gains ‹feat/Chakra Adept› as a bonus feat. She need not meet the feat's prerequisites." special="When the serpent-fire adept has used all her rounds of *chakra expertise* to maintain open chakras, if she does not spend additional ki to maintain her open chakras on the next round, her highest chakra closes and she can spend a @HLswift_action to use any one of the abilities of her remaining open chakras. On the round after that, all her chakras close, and if she wishes to reopen them, she must start again with her root chakra." replace="Bonus feat gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Linked Chakras (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power standard="Once per day, the serpent-fire adept can open multiple chakras at once. The round that she does this, she is required to make saving throws only to resist being overwhelmed by the highest chakra she opens, though the save DCs are increased by 5." incrementPlain="She can now do~7~5" incrementDesc=this incrementEnd=" times a day." replace="Wholeness of body"}

::ab[Chakra Mastery (Ex)]{icon=power l10="The serpent-fire adept gains ‹feat/Chakra Master› as a bonus feat. She need not meet the feat's prerequisites. When maintaining her open chakras with *chakra expertise,* she can spend 1 point of ki to gain the benefits of two chakras rather than one in that round (or three chakras, if she has opened her crown chakra)." l14="The serpent-fire adept's pool of serpent-fire ki increases to 7 points." l18="It increases to 9 points." replace="Bonus feats gained at 10th, 14th, and 18th level"}

sin_monk.name: Sin Monk
description: ## Sin Monk
‹SOURCE Antihero's Handbook/14›

Some vices prove too tempting even to those who have trained their minds and bodies against such corruptions, and the seven sins of ancient Thassilon stand among the most alluring. Martial experts who have allowed such passions to lead them astray may find themselves gaining powers drawn from their newly embraced sins.
@HL[This is an ex-class archetype and can be taken by a character immediately upon becoming an ex-monk.]

>The following archetype can be taken by an ex-monk immediately upon becoming an ex-monk, regardless of character level, replacing some or all of the lost class abilities. If another archetype the character had before she became an ex-monk replaces the same ability as the ex-class archetype, she loses the old archetype in favor of the new one; otherwise, she can retain both archetypes as normal. Sin monks can gain further levels in the monk class, even though becoming an ex-monk normally prohibits further advancement in the class. While an ex-member of a class can recant her failings and atone for her fall from her original class (typically involving an ‹spell/atonement› spell), her acceptance of her ex-class archetype means she must atone both for her initial fall and for further straying from the path. As a result, such a character must be the target of two atonement spells or a similar effect to regain her lost class features. Upon doing so, she immediately loses this archetype and regains her original class (and archetype, if she had one).

::ab[Well of Sin (Su)]{head l=4 icon=power ability="A sin monk gains a pool of sin points, representing energy he has gained from indulging in forbidden vices. The sin monk has a number of sin points equal to half his class level + his Wisdom modifier; these points cannot be used on abilities that require spending ki points." swift="He can spend 1 point from his sin pool to activate one of the following abilities until the start of his next turn." order=aw special="When a sin monk gains a level in the monk class and would gain one of the following class features, he can forfeit that class feature to instead increase the size of his sin pool by 1 sin point: *purity of body, diamond body, diamond soul, timeless body, tongue of the sun and moon.* Once the decision is made, it cannot be changed." replace="Ki pool~alters high jump"}
::ab[Envy]{next icon=boost passive="The sin monk gains a +4 bonus on Perception and Sense Motive checks." imp10="This bonus increases to +6." imp16="This bonus increases to +8."}

::ab[Gluttony]{next icon=aid ability="Each time the sin monk deals lethal damage with a melee attack, he regains a number of hit points equal to the amount of damage dealt (maximum 2). He can't exceed his maximum number of hit points." imp10="The maximum number of hit points he regains increases to 4." imp16="It increases to 6."}

::ab[Greed]{next icon=boost passive="The sin monk's attacks count as being either cold iron or silver (sin monk's choice) for the purpose of overcoming DR." imp10="He adamantine to the list of materials he can choose to have his attacks count as being for the purpose of overcoming DR." imp16="The sin monk's attacks also ignore hardness."}
::ab[Lust]{next icon=boost passive="The sin monk gains a +4 bonus on Bluff and Diplomacy checks." imp10="This bonus increases to +6." imp16="This bonus increases to +8."}

::ab[Pride]{next icon=def ability="The sin monk gains one illusory double of himself, which functions as mirror image. This double automatically disappears at the beginning of the sin monk's next turn unless destroyed first." imp10="The sin monk gains two such replicas of himself." imp16="He gains three."}

::ab[Sloth]{next icon=power ability="The sin monk gains the benefits of ‹feat/Vital Strike›." imp10="He also gains the benefits of ‹feat/Improved Vital Strike›." imp16="He also gains the benefits of ‹feat/Greater Vital Strike›."}

::ab[Wrath]{next icon=boost ability="The sin monk increases the save DCs of his attacks with ‹feat/Stunning Fist› (and ‹feat/Punishing Kick›, if he has that feat) by 1." imp10="The save DC instead increases by 2." imp16="It now increases by 3."}

::ab[Sinful Strike (Su)]{l=7 icon=boost swift="A sin monk can spend 2 sin pointsto focus his power. The next time he deals damage before the end of his next turn, he deals an additional amount damage equal to his monk level." replace="Wholeness of body"}

::ab[Burden with Sin (Su)]{l=12 icon=lower swift="A sin monk can spend one daily use of Stunning Fist to draw his sinful nature to the fore of his consciousness. The next person he hits with a melee attack before the end of his next turn must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + half the sin monk's class level + his Wisdom modifier) or suffer the burden of the sin monk's sins. The target is treated as if she were ‹rule/carrying a load› one step heavier (from a light load to a medium load, and so on) and must succeed at a caster level check to use any form of teleportation (DC = 10 + the monk's class level)." special="This is a curse effect that lasts a number of rounds equal to the sin monk's Wisdom modifier unless it is removed through ‹spell/remove curse› or a similar effect." replace="Abundant step"}
::ab[Simultaneous Sins (Su)]{l=19 icon=power swift="A sin monk can spend 3 points from his sin pool to gain the benefits of two different sins simultaneously." replace="Empty body"}

::ab[Spawn of Sin (Ex)]{l=20 icon=magic-def ability="The sin monk becomes the physical manifestation of his sins. He is forevermore treated as an ‹type/aberration› rather than a humanoid (or whatever the monk's creature type was) for the purpose of spells and magical effects, and his mind is so consumed by sin that he gains immunity to mind-affecting effects. He does not change in appearance or gain other abilities; however, should the sin monk be slain and brought back from the dead, he returns to life as a ‹monster/sinspawn›, retaining his class levels but otherwise replacing his creature type and racial abilities with those of his new form and becoming an NPC under the GM's control." replace="Perfect self"}

softstrike_monk.name: Softstrike Monk
description: ## Softstrike Monk
‹SOURCE Martial Arts Handbook/6›
The practices of softstrike monks date back to the peaceful eras of Imperial Lung Wa. Still common among warriors today, softstrike monks employ a nonlethal but effective form of combat.

::ab[Weapon Proficiency]{icon=power-down note="A softstrike monk is proficient with the ‹eq-weapon/aklys›, ‹eq-weapon/bolas›, ‹eq-weapon/club›, ‹eq-weapon/light flail›, ‹eq-weapon/light mace›, ‹eq-weapon/nunchaku›, ‹eq-weapon/quarterstaff›, ‹eq-weapon/sap›, ‹eq-weapon/sling›, and any weapon with the monk special quality that can deal bludgeoning damage. He is considered proficient only with monk weapons that can deal multiple types of damage when dealing bludgeoning damage." replace="Weapon proficiency"}

::ab[Feather Touch (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A softstrike monk does not take the normal -4 penalty on attack rolls for using a lethal weapon to deal nonlethal damage. In addition, he can use his *stunning fist* ability while making a nonlethal attack with a weapon."}

::ab[Nonlethal Strikes (Ex)]{flavor="A softstrike monk focuses on blows that do not inflict deadly harm." icon=melee-down ability="When a softstrike monk deals nonlethal damage with his unarmed strikes, his monk level is treated as 4 levels higher for the purposes of determining the damage he deals (to a maximum of 4d8 at 20th level for a Medium monk)." special="When dealing lethal damage with his unarmed strikes, his monk level is treated as if it were 4 levels lower (minimum 1st level) for the purpose of determining damage." alter="Unarmed strike"}

::ab[Tenet of Life (Su)]{icon=down passive="Though a softstrike monk accepts that death may happen in combat, he cannot abide killing in cold blood. If he slays an ‹misc/unconscious› or ‹misc/helpless› living foe, or merely stands by and allows such a killing to occur, he becomes ‹misc/shaken› for 1 hour. In addition, the act disrupts the softstrike monk's ki; if he has a *ki pool,* he loses a number of ki points equal to half his level, as if they had been spent."}

::ab[Life-Giving Blows (Su)]{l=6 icon=melee-lower flavor="A softstrike monk can force a piece of his life force into a nonliving creature to disrupt its animating essence." free="If he hits an undead foe with an unarmed attack, he can spend 1 ki point to remove the target's immunity to nonlethal damage for a number of minutes equal to his Wisdom modifier. If, during this time, the target's nonlethal damage exceeds its current hit points, it is destroyed." imp10="A softstrike monk can also use this ability against constructs." replace="Bonus feats gained at 6th and 10th levels"}

::ab[Wholeness of Body and Spirit (Su)]{l=7 icon=down-aid-def ability="A softstrike monk can use wholeness of body on both himself and other creatures, though it heals only nonlethal damage. If he uses this ability on an unconscious foe, he can spend 1 additional ki point to force the creature to accept a pact of life. The creature must succeed at a Will saving throw (DC = 10 + half the softstrike monk's level + his Wisdom modifier) or the pact renders it unable to directly attack the monk and any allies within 60 feet, as per ‹spell/sanctuary›, for a number of days equal to the softstrike monk's level." alter="Wholeness of body"}

::ab[Incapacitating Palm (Su)]{l=15 icon=down ability="A softstrike monk renders the target of his *quivering palm* unconscious instead of killing the creature. Upon failing its Fortitude saving throw, the target is knocked unconscious for a number of minutes equal to the softstrike monk's level." alter="Quivering palm"}

::ab[Resilient Body (Su)]{l=19 icon=def passive="A softstrike monk's mastery of nonlethal combat lets him apply his techniques to his defense. A softstrike monk converts any ‹misc/precision damage› he takes to nonlethal damage." replace="Empty body"}

softstrike_monk_uc.name: Softstrike Monk (Unchained)
regex: special="This ability only applies if the monk selects the *wholeness of body* ki power."
special="This ability only applies if the monk selects the *quivering palm* ki power."

sohei.name: Sohei
description: ## Sohei
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/60›

The sohei are masters of the horse and the hunt. A monastic soldier, the sohei is equally adept at combat afoot or on horseback. While skilled in unarmed techniques, many sohei favor weapons they can use while mounted on horseback or against others' horses.
::cskill{gain=ha}

::ab[Weapon and Armor Proficiency]{icon=power ability="A sohei is proficient with all simple and martial weapons and with light armor, instead of a monk's usual weapon and armor proficiencies." alter="Weapon and armor proficiencies"}
::ab[Bonus Feats]{icon=power ability="A sohei may select ‹feat/mounted combat› feats as bonus feats."}

::ab[Devoted Guardian (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power-boost-roll ability="A sohei can always act in a surprise round even if he does not notice his enemies, though he remains ‹rule/flat-footed› until he acts. In addition, a sohei gains a bonus on initiative rolls equal to 1/2 his monk level." imp20="At 20th level, a sohei's initiative roll is automatically a natural 20." replace="Stunning fist"}
::ab[Unarmed Strike]{icon=down note="A sohei's unarmed strike damage does not increase at 4th level and above."}

::ab[Monastic Mount (Su)]{l=4 icon=aid-protect-power ="A sohei may spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to grant his mount ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to twice his level for 1 hour per level. In addition, as long as the sohei and his mount are adjacent, including when mounted, the mount gains any of the following abilities the sohei possesses: *AC bonus, diamond soul, evasion, high jump, improved evasion, ki strike* (as long as the sohei has at least 1 point in his *ki pool*), *perfect self,* and *still mind.* When a sohei spends points from his *ki pool,* his mount gains the same benefits as the sohei." replace="Fast movement~increased unarmed damage"}

::ab[Ki Weapon (Su)]{l=4 icon=boost swift="A sohei may spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to grant any weapon he wields (including his unarmed strike) an enhancement bonus on attack and damage rolls until the end of his next turn. This bonus is equal to ‹misc/one-fourth› of his monk level." replace="Slow fall~abundant step"}

::ab[Weapon Training (Ex)]{l=6 icon=power ability="A sohei gains *weapon training* in one of the following ‹ability/weapon groups›, as the ‹class/fighter› class feature: bows, crossbows, monk weapons, polearms, spears, or thrown weapons." special="A sohei may use *flurry of blows* and *ki strike* with any weapon in which he has weapon training." repeat="He may select an additional group of weapons.~12~6" replace="Purity of body~diamond body~quivering palm~timeless body~tongue of sun and moon"}

soul_shepherd.name: Soul Shepherd
description: ## Soul Shepherd
‹SOURCE Pathfinder #139: The Dead Road/65›
Whether studying at the feet of psychopomps or merely imitating the creatures' philosophy and methods, soul shepherds believe in safeguarding the Cycle of Souls.

::ab[Calming Strike (Su)]{icon=melee-touch order=iau immediate="After hitting an incorporeal undead creature with an unarmed strike, a soul shepherd can attempt to calm the creature, encouraging it to move peacefully to the next stage of its metaphysical journey. The target must succeed at a Will save (DC = 10 + half soul shepherd's level + the soul shepherd's Wisdom modifier) or be calmed, as ‹spell/calm spirit›, for 1 minute." ability="The soul shepherd can use this ability against a haunt by making a touch attack against an AC of 10 + the haunt's CR. A haunt does not receive a saving throw against this ability, but the ability's duration changes to concentration + 1 round, up to 1 round per level." useL=monk useUnit=time replace="Stunning fist"}

::ab[Otherworldly Resilience (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A soul shepherd gains ‹rule/DR› 2/adamantine, cold resistance 5, and electricity resistance 5." imp9="This improves to DR 5/adamantine, cold resistance 10, and electricity resistance 10." replace="Evasion~improved evasion"}

::ab[Spirit Sense (Su)]{l=12 icon=power swift="A soul shepherd can spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to grant himself the ability to notice, locate, and distinguish between living and undead creatures within 30 feet, just as if he had a ‹subtype/psychopomp«'s› *spiritsense* ability, for a number of rounds equal to his Wisdom modifier (minimum 1)." replace="Abundant step"}

::ab[Yamaraj's Judgment (Sp)]{l=15 icon=aid standard="A soul shepherd can spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to attempt to free an enslaved soul from an effect that is imprisoning it, such as ‹spell/soul bind› or ‹spell/trap the soul›. This functions as a targeted ‹spell/dispel magic› spell with a caster level equal to the soul shepherd's level. He can spend an additional ki point when he activates this ability to gain a +4 bonus on the caster level check. If the soul is held prisoner by a creature or a non-spell effect, the soul shepherd frees it if his caster level check result is at least 11 + the creature or effect's CR." replace="Quivering palm"}

spirit_master.name: Spirit Master
description: ## Spirit Master
‹SOURCE Undead Slayer's Handbook/22›
Spirit masters are monks who specialize in combating the undead, laying their corpses to rest, and sending their souls to final judgment.
::ab[Resilient Soul (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A spirit master gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against necromancy spells and effects." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Spirit Combat (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost ability="A spirit master can charge his unarmed strike so it affects incorporeal creatures. The spirit master's unarmed strike deals half damage to incorporeal creatures, or full damage if it's a magic weapon (such as via an ‹magic-wondrous/amulet of mighty fists›). This benefit lasts for a number of rounds equal to the spirit master's class level." useMod=Wisdom3 replace="Maneuver training"}

::ab[Diamond Spirit (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def passive="A spirit master becomes fortified against the effects of ‹rule/energy drain›. When the spirit master gains a negative level, he does not take any penalties, his total hit points remain unchanged, and he is not considered lower level for the purpose of level-dependent variables." order=pa ability="Once per day when attempting a Fortitude save to remove a temporary negative level, the spirit master may choose to automatically succeed." special="The spirit master still dies if his number of negative levels equals or exceeds his number of Hit Dice." replace="Purity of body"}

::ab[Spirit Burst (Su)]{l=7 icon=aura free="Whenever a spirit master destroys an undead creature with a melee attack, he may expend 2 points from his *ki pool* to release an 15-foot-radius ‹misc/burst› of positive energy centered on the destroyed creature. Undead creatures in the affected area each take a number of points of damage equal the number of Hit Dice of the destroyed creature. Living creatures in the area are healed by the same amount." replace="Wholeness of body"}

::ab[Purifying Palm (Su)]{l=15 icon=melee-lower flavor="The spirit master gains the ability to alter the balance of positive and negative energy within an opponent's body." ability="The spirit master can make a purifying palm attack once per day, and must announce his intention before making a melee attack roll. If his attack deals damage, the target must attempt a Will save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the spirit master's class level + the spirit master's Wisdom modifier). On a failed save, the target cannot ‹ability/channel energy›, nor can it be healed by positive or negative energy. This effect lasts for 1 round per class level. If the target is an undead creature, it must succeed at its Will save or be instantly destroyed." replace="Quivering palm"}

::ab[Spirit Flow (Su)]{l=19 icon=def flavor="A spirit master can dramatically alter the flow of his own ki." immediate="The spirit master can expend 3 ki points to alter his ki for a number of rounds equal to his class level. During this time, he is healed by negative energy as if he were an undead creature. The spirit master is still healed by positive energy as normal." replace="Empty body"}

::ab[Spirit Mastery (Su)]{l=20 icon=def-magic passive="A spirit master gains DR 10/evil and becomes immune to ‹rule/ability drain« and ability damage›." order=pa ability="Once per week, the spirit master may bring a dead creature back to life. This ability functions as ‹spell/true resurrection›, except the spirit master may not resurrect a creature that has been dead for longer than 1 day per class level." replace="Perfect self"}

student_of_stone.name: Student of Stone
description: ## Student of Stone
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/146›
By following the path of the stone, students of stone give up much of monks' mobility in favor of sheer resilience. A student of stone has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Oread}

::ab[Hard as Stone (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="Whenever an opponent rolls to confirm a critical hit against a student of stone, treat the student of stone's AC as +4 higher than normal." replace=Evasion}

::ab[Strength of Stone (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost-def flavor="A student of stone learns to draw strength from the earth." passive="So long as both he and his opponent are touching the ground, the student of stone gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls, damage rolls, ‹rule/bull rush› and ‹rule/trip› combat maneuver rolls, and to his CMD when resisting a bull rush or trip attempt." replace="Fast movement"}

::ab[Bonus Feat]{l=6 icon=power l6="A student of stone adds ‹feat/Elemental Fist› to his list of available bonus feats. If the student of stone selects Elemental Fist as a bonus feat, he may only deal acid damage when using the feat." l10="The student of stone adds ‹feat/Shaitan Style› to his list of bonus feats." l14="He adds ‹feat/Shaitan Skin›." l18="He adds ‹feat/Shaitan Earthblast›." alter="Bonus feats"}

::ab[Bones of Stone (Su)]{l=7 icon=def swift="A student of stone can spend 1 ki point to gain DR 2/magic until the beginning of his next turn." imp10="He can spend 1 ki point to gain DR 2/chaotic until his next turn." imp15="He can spend 1 ki point to gain DR 5/chaotic until his next turn." replace="High jump"}
::ab[Body of Stone (Ex)]{l=9 icon=def passive="A student of stone gains the benefits of the ‹magic-enh/light fortification› armor property." replace="Improved evasion"}

::ab[Soul of Stone (Su)]{l=12 icon=power swift="A student of stone can spend 1 ki point to gain ‹umr/tremorsense› 15 feet until his next turn." imp16="The range of this tremorsense increases to 30 feet." replace="Abundant step"}

::ab[Stone Self]{l=20 icon=magic-def-power note="A student of stone becomes an ‹subtype/earth› ‹type/outsider›. He gains the earth subtype, as well as DR 5/chaotic, burrow speed 20 feet, and tremorsense 20 feet."}

terra_cotta_monk.name: Terra-Cotta Monk
description: ## Terra-Cotta Monk
‹SOURCE Dungeoneer's Handbook/22›
Possessing a natural affinity for all things earthen, terracotta monks are experts at turning the stone around them against their enemies.
::cskill{gain=kd~ke~ddev lose=kh~perf~ride}

::ab[Trap Intuition (Ex)]{l=2 icon=power-boost passive="Whenever a terra-cotta monk comes within 10 feet of a trap, he receives an immediate Perception check to notice the trap. The GM should make this check in secret. A terra-cotta monk gains a +2 bonus on Perception checks to notice traps. This bonus increases to +4 if the trap is located in a stone wall or floor." replace=Evasion}

::ab[Stone Grip (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost-power order=paw passive="A terra-cotta monk adds a bonus equal to his class level on all Climb checks." ability="He can move at his full movement rate while climbing with no increase to the check's DC." swift="By spending 1 point from his *ki pool,* a monk gains the benefits of ‹spell/spider climb› for 1 round." replace="High jump"}

::ab[Trap Dodge (Ex)]{l=10 icon=def passive="A terra-cotta monk gains a bonus equal to his Wisdom modifier on all saving throws made against effects produced by mechanical traps." replace="Improved evasion"}

::ab[Sudden Adit (Su)]{l=12 icon=magic move="A terra-cotta monk can create passages through stone or dirt as if using the spell ‹spell/passwall› with a caster level equal to his monk level. Using this ability consumes 2 points of the monk's *ki pool.*" replace="Abundant step"}

::ab[Petrifying Strike (Su)]{l=15 icon=lower ability="A terra-cotta monk can turn an opponent to stone with a single strike. He can use this ability once per day, and must announce the attempt before making his attack roll. If the terra-cotta monk's strike is successful, the creature must succeed at a Fortitude save (DC = 10 + 1/2 the monk's level + the monk's Wis modifier) or be ‹rule/petrified› for 1 hour per monk level. Incorporeal creatures and creatures immune to petrification are unaffected by this ability." replace="Quivering palm"}

::ab[Rainmaker (Su)]{l=19 icon=magic standard="Anytime a terra-cotta monk is underground, he may cause a section of the roof to come down on his enemies per the spell ‹spell/earthquake›, with a caster level equal to his monk level. The terra-cotta monk may use this ability only while in a cave, cavern, or tunnel. Using this ability consumes 3 points from his *ki pool.*" replace="Empty body"}

tetori.name: Tetori
description: ## Tetori
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/61›
The style of the tetori is that of the majestic wrestler - a warrior with a dizzying array of grabs, holds, and locks with which to bewilder and incapacitate his foes.

::ab[Bonus Feat]{icon=power ability="A tetori gains the following bonus feats." imp1="‹feat/Improved Grapple›" imp2="‹feat/Stunning Pin›" imp6="‹feat/Greater Grapple›" imp10="feat/Pinning Knockout›" imp14="‹feat/Chokehold›" imp18="‹feat/Neckbreaker›." replace="Bonus feats"}

::ab[Graceful Grappler (Ex)]{icon=boost-def ability="A tetori uses his monk level in place of his base attack bonus to determine CMB and CMD for grappling." imp4="He suffers no penalties on attack rolls, can make attacks of opportunity while grappling, and retains his Dexterity bonus to AC when pinning an opponent or when ‹rule/grappled›." imp8="A tetori gains the ‹umr/grab› special attack when using unarmed strikes, and can use this ability against creatures his own size or smaller by spending 1 point from his *ki pool,* or against larger creatures by spending 2 points from his *ki pool.*" imp15="A tetori gains the ‹umr/constrict› special attack, inflicting his unarmed strike damage on any successful ‹rule/grapple› check." replace="Flurry of blows"}

::ab[Counter-Grapple (Ex)]{l=4 icon=melee ability="A tetori wrestler may make an attack of opportunity against a creature attempting to grapple him. This ability does not allow the tetori to make an attack of opportunity against a creature with the Greater Grapple feat." imp6="He may use *counter-grapple* even if his attacker has ‹rule/concealment› or total concealment." imp8="He may *counter-grapple* even if he is flat-footed." imp10="He may *counter-grapple* even if his attacker has exceptional reach." replace="Slow fall"}

::ab[Break Free (Ex)]{l=5 icon=boost order=pi passive="A tetori adds his monk level on combat maneuver or Escape Artist checks made to escape a grapple." immediate="If a tetori fails a save against an effect that causes him to become ‹rule/entangled›, ‹rule/paralyzed›, ‹spell/slow«ed›, or ‹misc/staggered›, he may spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to attempt a new save." replace="High jump"}

::ab[Inescapable Grasp (Su)]{l=9 icon=lower swift="A tetori can spend 1 point from his *ki pool* to suppress his opponents' ‹spell/freedom of movement› and magical bonuses to Escape Artist or on checks to escape a grapple. This lasts until the beginning of the wrestler's next turn." imp13="This ability also duplicates the effect of ‹spell/dimensional anchor›." imp17="The tetori's unarmed strike gains the ‹magic-enh/ghost touch› special ability, and an ‹subtype/incorporeal› creature that he strikes gains the grappled condition (Reflex negates, DC 10 + 1/2 the wrestler's level + his Wisdom modifier)." replace="Abundant step~improved evasion~timeless body~tongue of sun and moon"}

::ab[Form Lock (Su)]{l=13 icon=magic ability="A tetori can negate a polymorph effect by touch with a Wisdom check, adding a bonus equal to his monk level, against a DC of 11 + the caster level of the effect (or HD of the creature, for supernatural polymorph effects). This is a @HLstandard_action requiring 2 points from the tetori's *ki pool,* or an @HLimmediate_action if a creature the tetori is grappling attempts to use a polymorph effect." replace="Diamond soul"}

::ab[Iron Body (Su)]{l=19 icon=def-boost-down move="A tetori can make his tissues ultra-dense for 1 minute as the ‹spell/iron body› spell by spending 3 points from his *ki pool.*" replace="Empty body"}

treetop_monk.name: Treetop Monk
description: ## Treetop Monk
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/206›

While many vanaras follow traditional monastic training and traditions, others learn to blend exotic combat and the mysterious forces of ki with the natural world, allowing them to move through trees and overgrowth to deliver devastating attacks. A treetop monk has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Vanara}
::ab[Branch Runner (Ex)]{l=3 icon=boost passive="A treetop monk adds half the base speed bonus from his fast movement ability to his racial climb speed." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Wood Affinity (Su)]{l=5 icon=power free="A treetop monk may expend 1 point from his *ki pool* to treat a wooden object as if it were not broken for 1 minute (this includes a weapon with a wooden haft such as an axe or spear)." imp8="As a @HLfree_action, a treetop monk can expend 1 point from his *ki pool* to use the ‹feat/Lunge› feat with any wooden or wood-hafted melee weapon." replace="Purity of body"}
::ab[Freedom of Movement (Su)]{l=12 icon=def swift="A treetop may expend 1 point from his *ki pool* to gain the effects of ‹spell/freedom of movement› for 1 round." replace="Abundant step"}

underfoot_adept.name: Underfoot Adept
description: ## Underfoot Adept
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/65›

An underfoot adept turns his diminutive stature and unorthodox footwork into a powerful weapon. Effortlessly moving across the battlefield, he ducks under the legs of larger creatures and then topples them with surprising attacks. An underfoot adept has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Halfling}

::ab[Underfoot Grace (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def flavor="An underfoot adept uses his size and grace to avoid the attacks of those he passes." ability="When using the Acrobatics skill to avoid attacks of opportunity by moving through a threatened area or an enemy's space, he only takes a -5 penalty when doing so at full speed, instead of the normal -10 penalty." replace="Bonus feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Underfoot Trip (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power flavor="An underfoot adept learns a number of maneuvers and grabs that can cause even the largest opponents to stumble and fall." ability="He gains ‹feat/Improved Trip› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the requirements." imp4="He acts as if he is one size larger for the purposes of determining the maximum size of creatures he can trip and when determining his CMB and CMD for purposes of a ‹rule/trip› combat maneuver." incrementPlain="He now acts as if he~4~4" incrementDesc=is incrementEnd=" sizes larger." replace="Stunning fist"}

::ab[Improved Underfoot Grace (Ex)]{l=5 icon=def flavor="An underfoot adept's ability to avoid attacks of opportunity against those he passes improves." ability="When using the Acrobatics skill to avoid attacks of opportunity, while moving through a threatened area or through an enemy's space, he takes no penalty when doing so at full speed." replace="High jump"}

wanderer.name: Wanderer
description: ## Wanderer
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/76›

Some monks wander the world in humility to learn and to share wisdom and philosophy from their teachers with those they meet, often aiding those who are in need. A wanderer has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Human}
::cskill{gain=diplo~kg~kl~ling~sur}

::ab[Far Traveler (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="The wanderer gains either one additional language known or proficiency in one exotic or martial weapon." imp4="The wanderer may gain an additional language known or may retrain her weapon proficiency from this ability to a different exotic or martial weapon." repeat="She may gain a language or retrain again.~4~4" replace="Bonus feat gained at 1st level"}

::ab[Long Walk (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-boost-def ability="The wanderer gains ‹feat/Endurance› as a bonus feat, and the feat bonus doubles when he makes Constitution checks because of a forced march. In addition, a wanderer gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against spells and effects that cause exhaustion and fatigue." replace="Still mind"}

::ab[Light Step (Su)]{l=5 icon=def-magic passive="A wanderer leaves no trail and cannot be tracked, though he can leave a trail if desired." order=pa ability="By spending 1 point from his *ki pool,* he can use ‹spell/ant haul›, ‹spell/feather step›, ‹spell/pass without trace›, or ‹spell/tireless pursuit› as a spell-like ability (with a caster level equal to his monk level)." replace="Slow fall"}

::ab[Inscrutable (Su)]{l=5 icon=def passive="The wanderer gains a supernatural air of mystery. The DC to gain information or insight into the wanderer with Diplomacy, Knowledge skills, or Sense Motive increases by 5." order=pa ability="By spending 1 point from his *ki pool,* the wanderer gains ‹spell/nondetection› for 24 hours with a caster level equal to his monk level." replace="High jump"}

::ab[Wanderer's Wisdom (Ex)]{l=7 icon=boost flavor="The wanderer can dispense excellent advice in the form of philosophical proverbs and parables." swift="The wanderer can ‹performance/inspire courage› or ‹performance/inspire competence› as a ‹class/bard› of his monk level by spending 2 points from his *ki pool.* This affects one creature within 30 feet and lasts a number of rounds equal to the wanderer's Wisdom modifier (minimum 1 round). This ability is language-dependent." replace="Wholeness of body"}

::ab[Disappear Unnoticed (Ex)]{l=12 icon=def ability="The wanderer may use Stealth to hide even while being directly observed or when no cover or concealment is available, as long as he is adjacent to at least one creature of his size or larger, by spending 1 point from his *ki pool.* This effect lasts until the beginning of the wanderer's next turn and may be continued in consecutive rounds by spending 1 ki point each round." replace="Abundant step"}
::ab[Free Step (Su)]{l=13 icon=def passive="The wanderer gains continuous ‹spell/freedom of movement› as a continuous spell-like ability." replace="Diamond soul"}

wasteland_meditant.name: Wasteland Meditant
description: ## Wasteland Meditant
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/64›
Some monks journey to remote and isolated corners of the world to find serenity and a heightened sense of awareness.

**Dehydrating Strike (Su):** As a free action as part of a melee attack, a wasteland meditant can draw the moisture out of a living creature damaged by his unarmed strike. Unless the creature succeeds at a Will save (DC = 10 + half the monk's level + his Wisdom modifier), the creature is fatigued for a number of rounds equal to half the wasteland meditant's level (minimum 1). At 5th level, the creature struck is also staggered for 1 round if it fails its save. At 10th level, a creature that fails its save is exhausted instead of fatigued.

When used against a plant creature or a creature with the water subtype, dehydrating strike inflicts the above conditions (as applicable) on a failed save and also deals 1d6 points of damage, plus 1d6 additional points of damage at 4th level and every 4 levels thereafter. This damage is in addition to the wasteland meditant's normal unarmed strike damage. A wasteland meditant can use this ability a number of times per day equal to 3 + his Wisdom modifier but only once per round.
Creatures immune to critical hits or ‹misc/precision damage› are immune to this ability.
This replaces @HL[stunning fist].
**Vigor (Ex):** At 2nd level, when a wasteland meditant succeeds at a Fortitude save against an effect with a partial effect on a successful save, he instead suffers no effect.
This replaces @HL[evasion].

**Desert Strider (Ex):** At 4th level, a wasteland meditant ignores movement penalties for ‹rule/difficult terrain› when in desert terrain. Additionally, he kicks up dust around his body whenever he performs a double move action in desert terrain. This dust grants the wasteland meditant ‹rule/concealment›. The miss chance granted by this concealment starts at 10% at 4th level and increases by 10% at 6th level and every 2 levels thereafter, to a maximum of 80% at 18th level. This concealment lasts until the start of the meditant's next turn and stacks with any concealment provided by movement, such as from the ‹feat/Wind Stance› feat.
This replaces @HL[slow fall].

**Improved Vigor (Ex):** At 9th level, a wasteland meditant's vigor ability improves. He still takes no damage on a successful Fortitude saving throw against attacks, but henceforth he takes only half damage on a failed save.
This replaces @HL[improved evasion].

**Pillar of Salt (Su):** At 15th level, a wasteland meditant can transform a creature's body into a column of crumbling salt with a touch. He can use pillar of salt once per day as part of an unarmed strike, and he must announce his intent before making his attack roll. Creatures immune to critical hits or transmutation effects cannot be affected. A creature successfully damaged by the wasteland meditant's unarmed strike must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + half the monk's level + his Wisdom modifier) or begin to calcify. Each round thereafter, the target of pillar of salt takes 2 points of ‹rule/Strength and »Dexterity drain› and gains a +1 bonus to its natural armor as a crust of salt begins to cover its body and restrict movement. These effects take place every round at the start of the wasteland meditant's turn and continue for 1 round per level the wasteland meditant has. If the target is reduced to 0 Strength or Dexterity at any time during this duration, the creature is instantly killed and its body is transformed into a pillar of salt. A creature killed in this way can't be brought back to life except by ‹spell/miracle›, ‹spell/true resurrection›, or ‹spell/wish›. Total immersion in water suppresses the ongoing effects of pillar of salt but resets the accumulated natural armor bonus to zero. As long as the target lives, the ongoing effect of pillar of salt can be ended via a heal spell.
This replaces @HL[quivering palm].

water_dancer.name: Water Dancer
description: ## Water Dancer
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/65›
Water dancers derive their martial training from ancient nereid traditions jealously guarded by these enigmatic fey.

**Elemental Focus (Su and Sp):** A water dancer gains the elemental focus ability of the ‹class/kineticist› class, but must choose water as his ‹ability/element<s›. He gains the ‹kinetic/basic hydrokinesis› wild talent as normal, and gains the ‹kinetic/water>kinetic» blast› feature of the kineticist class for the water element, using his monk level as his effective kineticist level. He can't use his kinetic blast when armored or encumbered.
This replaces @HL[flurry of blows], @HL[stunning fist], and @HL[quivering palm].

**Nereid's Grace (Su):** When unarmored and unencumbered, a water dancer adds 1 point of Charisma bonus per monk level to his Armor Class as a dodge bonus. If he is caught flat-footed or otherwise denied his Dexterity bonus, he also loses this bonus. He uses his Charisma score instead of his Wisdom score to determine the size of his ki pool and the DC and effects of monk class features.
This replaces the @HL[bonus feat] the monk normally gains at 1st level.
**Unarmed Strike:** A water dancer gains the unarmed strike monk class feature but treats his unarmed strike damage as that of a monk 4 levels lower (minimum 1st level).
This alters @HL[unarmed strike].
**Burn (Ex):** At 2nd level, a water dancer gains the burn class feature of the kineticist class.
This replaces @HL[evasion].

**Wild Talents:** At 2nd level and every 4 levels thereafter, a water dancer selects a new ‹ability/utility wild talent<s› from the list of options available to the kineticist class, treating his monk level - 2 (minimum 1) as his kineticist level for the purpose of fulfilling prerequisites as well as for level-dependent effects of the utility talent. He can select only universal wild talents or those that match his element (see elemental focus above). At 6th, 10th, and 16th levels, he can replace one of his utility wild talents with another wild talent of the same level or lower. He can't replace a wild talent that he used to qualify for another of his wild talents.
This replaces the @HL[bonus feats] at 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th levels.

**Water Dance (Su):** At 4th level, a water dancer can move across any liquid surface as though it were solid ground up to a maximum distance of 20 feet before falling through the liquid's surface as normal. This distance increases by 10 feet at 6th level and every 2 levels thereafter, to a maximum of 90 feet at 18th level. Additionally, at 10th level the water dancer can even move vertically up water surfaces (such as a flowing waterfall) as though under the effects of ‹spell/spider climb›. If he runs out of movement before reaching a solid surface when climbing a vertical water source, he immediately falls.
This replaces @HL[slow fall].
**Metakinesis (Su):** At 9th level, a water dancer gains the ability to alter his kinetic blasts as the kineticist's metakinesis (empower) class feature.
This replaces @HL[improved evasion].

**Water Stride (Sp):** At 12th level, a water dancer can leap from one source of water to another as per ‹spell/dimension door› as a move action by expending 1 point of ki from his ki pool. He must begin and end his movement in a space of water equal to his size. He cannot take other creatures with him when he uses this ability.
This replaces @HL[abundant step].

weapon_adept.name: Weapon Adept
description: ## Weapon Adept
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/114›

While all monks train in both unarmed combat and with weapons, the weapon adept seeks to become one with his weapons, transforming them into perfect extensions of his own body. Through such training, a weapon adept seeks to attain perfection by becoming a living weapon himself. A weapon adept has the following class features.

**Perfect Strike (Ex):** At 1st level, a weapon adept gains ‹feat/Perfect Strike› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites. At 10th level, the monk can roll his attack roll three times and take the higher result. If one of these rolls is a critical threat, he can choose which one of his other two rolls to use as his confirmation roll.
This ability replaces @HL[Stunning Fist].

**Way of the Weapon Master (Ex):** At 2nd level, a weapon adept gains ‹feat/Weapon Focus› as a bonus feat with one of his monk weapons. At 6th level, the monk gains ‹feat/Weapon Specialization› with the same weapon as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites.
This ability replaces @HL[evasion].
**Evasion (Ex):** At 9th level, the monk gains ‹ability/evasion›.
This ability replaces @HL[improved evasion].
**Uncanny Initiative (Ex):** At 17th level, a weapon adept does not need to roll for initiative. He always treats his initiative roll as if it resulted in any number of his choosing (from 1 to 20).
This ability replaces @HL[timeless body].

**Pure Power:** At 20th level, a weapon adept forsakes the ideals of the perfect self to become a bastion of the physical and mental virtues monks hold dear. The monk gains a +2 bonus to Strength, Dexterity, and Wisdom.
This ability replaces @HL[perfect self].

wildcat.name: Wildcat
description: ## Wildcat
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/105›

A wildcat is a student of the school of hard knocks, who dedicates himself to learning how to take down foes by any means necessary. A wildcat isn't afraid to smash a tankard over a foe's head, stomp an opponent's foot, gouge an eye, or generally create mayhem to gain any possible advantage.
**Ready for Anything (Ex):** At 3rd level, a wildcat gains a +2 bonus on initiative checks and Perception checks to act in a surprise round.
This ability replaces @HL[still mind].

**Brawler Maneuver Training (Ex):** At 4th level, a wildcat gains additional training with the ‹rule/dirty trick› combat maneuver. He gains a +1 bonus on combat maneuver checks when attempting this combat maneuver and a +1 bonus to his CMD when defending against this maneuver.

At 7th, 10th, and 16th levels, a wildcat becomes further trained in another combat maneuver, gaining the above +1 bonus on combat maneuver checks and to CMD. In addition, the bonuses granted by previous maneuver training increase by 1 each. For example, when a wildcat reaches 7th level, he gains a +1 bonus on one type of combat maneuver, +1 to her CMD against that combat maneuver, and the bonuses for the dirty trick combat maneuver increase to +2.
This ability replaces @HL[ki pool] and @HL[high jump].

**Improvised Weapon Mastery (Ex):** Starting at 4th level, a wildcat's damage with improvised weapons increases. When wielding an ‹misc/improvised weapon›, he uses the unarmed strike damage of a monk four levels lower instead of the base damage for that weapon (minimum monk level 1). For example, a 6th-level Medium wildcat wielding a broken bottle deals 1d6 points of damage instead of the weapon's normal 1d4. If the weapon normally deals more damage than this, its damage is unchanged. This increase in damage does not affect any other aspect of the weapon. The wildcat can decide to use the weapon's base damage instead of his adjusted unarmed strike damage. This must be declared before the attack roll is attempted.
This ability replaces all instances of @HL[slow fall].

**Bonus Feat:** A wildcat adds the following feats to his list of bonus feats at 6th level: ‹feat/Improved Dirty Trick›, ‹feat/Improved Reposition›, ‹feat/Improved Steal›. He adds the following feats to his list of bonus feats at 10th level: ‹feat/Quick Dirty Trick›, ‹feat/Quick Reposition›, ‹feat/Quick Steal›.

**Knockout (Ex):** Starting at 9th level, once per day a wildcat can unleash a devastating attack that can instantly knock a target unconscious. He must announce this intent before making his attack roll. If the wildcat's strike is successful and the target takes damage from the blow, the target must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + 1/2 the wildcat's level + the higher of the wildcat's Strength or Dexterity modifier) or fall unconscious for 1d6 rounds. Each round on its turn, the unconscious target can attempt a new saving throw to end the effect; this is a full-round action that does not provoke attacks of opportunity. Creatures immune to critical hits or nonlethal damage are immune to this ability. At 12th level, a wildcat can use this ability twice per day.
This ability replaces @HL[improved evasion] and @HL[abundant step].
**Turn the Tables (Ex):** At 13th level, opponents provoke an attack of opportunity from the wildcat whenever they fail at a combat maneuver against the wildcat.
This ability replaces @HL[diamond soul].
**Dirty Blow (Ex):** At 19th level, when a wildcat succeeds at a dirty trick combat maneuver, he can deal his unarmed strike damage to that opponent.
This ability replaces @HL[empty body].

windstep_master.name: Windstep Master
description: ## Windstep Master
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/17›

Most monks are agile and fleet of foot, but few can rival a windstep master's lightness of step. Windstep masters train in mountaintop monasteries and other holy houses dedicated to the wind, such as the Monastery of Unfolding Wind in Niswan.

**Hurricane Punch (Ex):** At 1st level, a windstep master gains ‹feat/Hurricane Punch› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites. While using this feat, he doesn't provoke an attack of opportunity, even if he doesn't have the ‹feat/Improved Bull Rush› feat.

At 4th level, while using the Hurricane Punch feat, the windstep master can ‹rule/bull rush› a target up to two size categories larger. At 12th level, the windstep master's target can be up to three size categories larger. At 20th level, the target can be of any size category.

At 8th level, when the windstep master uses the Hurricane Punch feat to successfully bull rush a creature, he can push the creature back an additional 5 feet. At 16th level, he can push the creature back an additional 10 feet.
This ability replaces @HL[stunning fist].

::ab[Wind Step (Su)]{l=4 flavor="The windstep master's steps become supernaturally light." move="He can use ‹spell/air walk› (as per the spell) up to a distance equal to his fast movement bonus. He must reach a solid, level surface able to support his weight by the end of his turn or he falls." icon=power replace="Slow fall"}

**Swift Ki (Su):** At 4th level, the windstep master can spend 1 point from his ki pool as a swift action to sustain his wind step for 1 minute. During this time, the windstep master can remain airborne even if he doesn't reach a solid surface. He can also spend 1 point from his ki pool as a swift action to increase his wind step distance by 20 feet for 1 round. The windstep master does not gain the ability to spend ki to increase his base speed by 20 feet for 1 round.
This ability alters @HL[ki pool].

zen_archer.name: Zen Archer
description: ## Zen Archer
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/115›

Some monks seek to become one with another weapon entirely - the bow. The zen archer takes a weapon most other monks eschew and seeks perfection in the pull of a taut bowstring, the flex of a bow's limbs, and the flight of an arrow fired true.

**Weapon and Armor Proficiency:** Zen archers are proficient with ‹eq-weapon/longbow«s›, ‹eq-weapon/shortbow«s›, ‹eq-weapon/composite longbow«s›, and ‹eq-weapon/composite shortbow«s› in addition to their normal weapon proficiencies.

**Flurry of Blows (Ex):** Starting at 1st level, a zen archer can make a flurry of blows as a full-attack action, but only when using a bow (even though it is a ranged weapon). He may not make a flurry of blows with his unarmed attacks or any other weapons. A zen archer does not apply his Strength bonus on damage rolls made with flurry of blows unless he is using a composite bow with a Strength rating. A zen archer's flurry of blows otherwise functions as normal for a monk of his level.
**A zen archer cannot use ‹feat/Rapid Shot›** or ‹feat/Manyshot› when making a flurry of blows with his bow.

**Bonus Feats:** A zen archer's bonus feats must be taken from the following list: ‹feat/Combat Reflexes›, ‹feat/Deflect Arrows›, ‹feat/Dodge›, ‹feat/Far Shot›, ‹feat/Point-Blank Shot›, ‹feat/Precise Shot›, and Rapid Shot. At 6th level, the following feats are added to the list: ‹feat/Focused Shot›, ‹feat/Improved Precise Shot›, Manyshot, ‹feat/Mobility›, and ‹feat/Parting Shot›. At 10th level, the following feats are added to the list: ‹feat/Improved Critical›, ‹feat/Pinpoint Targeting›, ‹feat/Shot on the Run›, and ‹feat/Snatch Arrows›. A monk need not have any of the prerequisites normally required for these feats to select them. These feats replace the monk's normal bonus feats.

**Perfect Strike (Ex):** At 1st level, a zen archer gains ‹feat/Perfect Strike› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites. A zen archer can use Perfect Strike with any bow. At 10th level, the monk can roll his attack roll three times and take the highest result. If one of these rolls is a critical threat, the monk must choose one of his other two rolls to use as his confirmation roll.
This ability replaces @HL[Stunning Fist].

**Way of the Bow (Ex):** At 2nd level, a zen archer gains ‹feat/Weapon Focus› as a bonus feat with one type of bow. At 6th level, the monk gains ‹feat/Weapon Specialization› with the same weapon as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites.
This ability replaces @HL[evasion].
**Zen Archery (Ex):** At 3rd level, a zen archer may use his Wisdom modifier instead of his Dexterity modifier on ranged attack rolls when using a bow.
This ability replaces @HL[maneuver training].
**Point Blank Master (Ex):** At 3rd level, a zen archer gains ‹feat/Point-Blank Master› as a bonus feat, even if he does not meet the prerequisites.
This ability replaces @HL[still mind].
**Ki Pool (Su):** At 4th level, in addition to the normal abilities of his ki pool, a zen archer may spend 1 point from his ki pool to increase the range increment for his bow by 50 feet for 1 round.

**Ki Arrows (Su):** At 5th level, a zen archer may spend 1 point from his ki pool as a swift action to change the damage dice of arrows he shoots to that of his unarmed strikes. This lasts until the start of his next turn. For example, a Medium zen archer's short bow normally deals 1d6 damage; using this ability, his arrows deal 1d8 damage until the start of his next turn.
This ability replaces @HL[purity of body].

**Reflexive Shot (Ex):** At 9th level, a zen archer can make attacks of opportunity with arrows from his bow. The monk still threatens squares he could reach with unarmed strikes, and can still only make one attack of opportunity per round (unless he has Combat Reflexes).
This ability replaces @HL[improved evasion].

**Trick Shot (Su):** At 11th level, a zen archer may hit targets that he might otherwise miss. By spending 1 point from his ki pool as a swift action, the zen archer can ignore ‹rule/concealment›. By spending 2 points, he can ignore total concealment or ‹rule/cover›. By spending 3 points, he can ignore total cover, even firing arrows around corners. The arrow must still be able to reach the target; a target inside a closed building with no open doors or windows cannot be attacked. These effects last for 1 round.
This ability replaces @HL[diamond body].

**Ki Focus Bow (Su):** At 17th level, as long as he has at least 1 point of ki in his ki pool, a zen archer may treat arrows fired from his bow as if they were ‹magic-enh/ki focus› weapons, allowing him to use his special ki attacks as if his arrows were unarmed attacks.
This ability replaces @HL[tongue of the sun and moon].
